"Both 'ER' and 'Grey's Anatomy' possess significant cultural legacies that invite comparative analysis with subsequent medical dramas. Occasionally, attempts are made to fuse these two approaches, though such efforts can be unsuccessful. Complications arise when Dr. Griffith regains consciousness and expresses a desire to resume surgical practice, necessitating supervision by his daughter. CHARLOTTESVILLE, Va. – Following a highly successful season, marking the most victories in over a decade, the University of Virginia softball program has officially announced its 2023 schedule, with season tickets now available for purchase. The schedule comprises 27 home games, which include two home tournaments and four home series within the Atlantic Coast Conference (ACC) framework. 

SEASON TICKET INFORMATION 
Beginning Tuesday, October 18, new season tickets for the 2023 season will be available, with general admission seats priced at $50 each. Reserved seating is contingent upon the renewal of current season ticket holders. Tickets can be acquired online through UVATix.com or by contacting the Virginia Athletic Ticket Office at (434) 924-8821. Furthermore, full-time faculty and staff at the University of Virginia are entitled to a 20 percent discount on the purchase of up to four season tickets by reaching out to the ticket office. Current season ticket holders may renew their 2022 seats starting on Tuesday, October 18, via UVATix.com or by calling the aforementioned ticket office. query: When does the 2022 Olympic Winter Games end?
positive: The 2022 Winter Olympics, officially designated as the XXIV Olympic Winter Games (Chinese: 第二十四届冬季奥林匹克运动会; pinyin: Dì Èrshísì Jiè Dōngjì Àolínpǐkè Yùndònghuì) and commonly referred to as Beijing 2022, constituted an international multi-sport event centered on winter sports, conducted from February 4 to February 20, 2022, in Beijing, China, and adjacent regions, with certain competitions commencing on February 2, 2022. This event represented the 24th iteration of the Winter Olympic Games. Beijing was awarded the status of host city in 2015 during the 128th session of the International Olympic Committee (IOC) held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, thereby marking its second occasion as an Olympic host. Furthermore, this edition of the Olympics was the final installment in a sequence of three consecutive Olympic events hosted in East Asia, following the 2018 Winter Olympics in Pyeongchang County, South Korea, and the 2020 Summer Olympics in Tokyo, Japan. Beijing's previous hosting of the 2008 Summer Olympics rendered it the first city to have hosted both the Summer and Winter Olympic Games.

The closing ceremony of the 2022 Winter Olympics took place at the Beijing National Stadium on February 20, 2022. This event featured a cultural presentation, closing remarks, and the formal handover to Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo, which are designated as the hosts for the 2026 Winter Olympics. The 2022 Winter Olympics encompassed a record total of 109 events across 15 disciplines within seven sports. Notably, there were seven new medal events introduced, which included men's and women's big air freestyle, women's monobob, mixed team competitions in freestyle skiing aerials, ski jumping, and snowboard cross, as well as the mixed relay in short track speed skating. The numbers in parentheses denote the quantity of medal events contested within each discipline. In October 2016, the International Ski Federation (FIS) announced initiatives to permit women's competitions in Nordic combined, thereby facilitating the first participation of this discipline at the Olympic level in Beijing. Subsequently, in November 2017, the FIS proposed an additional three events for potential Olympic inclusion: a ski jumping mixed team competition, along with men's and women's big air events in freestyle skiing.

Under the theme "Joyful Rendezvous upon Pure Ice and Snow," the Beijing 2022 Games sought to capitalize on the legacy of the Beijing 2008 Olympic Games and aimed to inspire 300 million individuals across China to engage in winter sports. The Games featured 109 events across seven Olympic winter sports, which were conducted in three distinct competition zones: central Beijing, Yanqing, and Zhangjiakou. Beijing was responsible for hosting four snow events (snowboard big air and freestyle skiing big air for both men and women), as well as all ice events (curling, ice hockey, and skating), utilizing the existing venues established during the Beijing 2008 Games. Yanqing, located 80 kilometers northwest of central Beijing and recognized for its proximity to the renowned Badaling and Juyongguan sections of the Great Wall, served as the venue for Alpine skiing and sliding events (bobsleigh, skeleton, and luge). In contrast, Zhangjiakou, situated further northwest in the neighboring Hebei province, hosted the remaining snow events (snowboard, freestyle skiing, cross-country skiing, ski jumping, Nordic combined, and biathlon) in its Chongli district, an area known for its popularity as a skiing destination.

positive: The upcoming Spanish Super Cup final marks the eighth encounter between the two prominent Spanish football clubs, Real Madrid and Barcelona, with Real Madrid having secured victory in six instances and suffered defeat in one. query: Who are the recipients of the 2022 Ivan Allen Jr. Prize for Social Courage?
positive: On January 21, 2022, Ford Greene, Ralph Long Jr., and Lawrence Williams, recognized as Georgia Tech’s first Black students, along with Ronald Yancey, the institution’s first Black graduate, are set to be awarded the 2022 Ivan Allen Jr. Prize for Social Courage. This prize, established in 2010, serves to honor the legacy of Ivan Allen Jr., a Georgia Tech alumnus and former Mayor of Atlanta. It is perpetually funded by a grant from the Wilbur and Hilda Glenn Family Foundation and includes a stipend of $100,000 for the recipients. The inaugural award was presented in March 2011, aiming to recognize exemplary leaders who demonstrate courage akin to that of Mayor Allen, particularly those who assume personal risks to enhance the lives of others, as articulated by Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera. He noted, “With great determination, Ford Greene, Ralph Long Jr., Lawrence Williams, and Ronald Yancey withstood hazards and adversity of every kind to prove to the world that Black students had the right to study at Georgia Tech, paving the way for the thousands of Black Yellow Jackets who have earned their degrees here since.” Greene, Long, and Williams, referred to as the “three pioneers” in the sculpture located in Harrison Square, began their studies in the fall of 1961. Their enrollment, while not met with violence as experienced at other southern universities, presented significant challenges.

Prominent figures such as House of Representatives member John Lewis and former U.S. President and Georgia Tech alumnus Jimmy Carter exemplify the notable individuals associated with this recognition. Although it is somewhat atypical for the nomination committee and the President of Georgia Tech to bestow this honor upon a student of the Institute, this decision is timely, coinciding with the 60th anniversary of Black student enrollment at Georgia Tech. The Institute acknowledges that its diversity constitutes a significant strength. The contributions of Ford Greene, Ralph Long Jr., the late Lawrence Williams, and Ronald Yancey during their tenure at Georgia Tech are indicative of their social courage, which continues to influence the campus environment to the present day. Their legacies have facilitated opportunities for subsequent generations of Black Yellow Jackets. An award symposium in honor of these four pioneers is scheduled for April 20, 2022, from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. in the Biltmore Ballroom located in Tech Square.

Ford Greene, Ralph Long Jr., and Lawrence Williams, the inaugural Black students at the Georgia Institute of Technology, along with Ronald Yancey, the institution's first Black graduate, are set to receive the 2022 Ivan Allen Jr. Prize for Social Courage. Their likenesses are permanently represented in campus sculptures; however, decades after their significant contributions towards dismantling racial barriers at Georgia Tech, they will be honored alongside previous recipients such as Jimmy and Rosalynn Carter, John Lewis, and Andrew Young, among other notable leaders. Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera stated, “The Ivan Allen Jr. Prize recognizes exemplary, courageous leaders—those who, like Mayor Allen, take on personal risks in an effort to improve the lives of others.” He further remarked that Greene, Long, Williams, and Yancey demonstrated remarkable resilience in facing various challenges to affirm that Black students possess the right to pursue education at Georgia Tech, thus facilitating the academic journeys of countless Black Yellow Jackets who have subsequently attained their degrees. Greene, Long, and Williams, referred to as the “three pioneers” in the Harrison Square sculpture commemorating their first day at the institution, commenced their studies in the fall of 1961. Although their enrollment did not provoke violence, as witnessed at other southern universities, it nevertheless presented significant challenges.

In 1961, three Black students commenced their studies at Georgia Tech, thereby establishing a significant milestone as the Institute became the first higher education institution in the Deep South to achieve peaceful integration without a court mandate. This period was characterized by widespread civil unrest across college campuses in the Southern United States. In acknowledgment of their pioneering contributions, Georgia Tech's inaugural Black students—Lawrence Williams, Ralph Long Jr., and the late Ford Greene, along with the first Black graduate Ronald Yancey—were honored with the 2022 Ivan Allen Jr. Prize for Social Courage on April 20 at the Biltmore Ballroom in Atlanta. A symposium, facilitated by 2020 Ivan Allen Jr. Prize recipient Charlayne Hunter-Gault, featured discussions among Long Jr., Williams, and Yancey regarding the integration of the Institute, their individual experiences, and the ongoing challenges related to racial equity and inclusion within higher education institutions nationwide. During the panel, the three former students recounted instances of racism they faced, despite the appearance of peaceful integration. Although these experiences rendered them socially and intellectually isolated from the majority of the student body at that time, the pioneers articulated a desire for future generations of Black students at Georgia Tech to feel a sense of belonging on campus. Williams expressed gratitude for those who continue to enroll and uphold the legacy of their contributions. Additionally, a video message from Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens, a graduate of Georgia Tech in chemical engineering, was presented during the event.

Student nominees are expected to provide evidence of leadership, engagement, and academic excellence. Nominations may be submitted by faculty members, academic advisors, or department chairs. Students who wish to self-nominate are required to obtain a letter of support from a faculty member, academic advisor, or department chair. query: What is the name of Meta's AI supercomputer?
positive: Meta has announced the launch of its AI Research SuperCluster (RSC) supercomputer, which is designed to enhance the pace of artificial intelligence research and facilitate the development of the metaverse. The RSC is intended to support the creation of advanced AI models that operate across a multitude of languages and contribute to the development of innovative augmented reality tools. The advancement of next-generation AI necessitates the use of powerful computational systems capable of executing quintillions of operations per second. Researchers at Meta have commenced utilizing the RSC to train extensive models in natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision, with the objective of eventually training models that encompass trillions of parameters across various Meta business applications. These applications range from content moderation algorithms aimed at detecting hate speech on platforms such as Facebook and Instagram to augmented reality functionalities anticipated to be integrated into the metaverse. The RSC possesses the capability to train models that analyze multimodal signals to assess whether an action, sound, or image is harmful or benign. Meta asserts that this initiative will not only enhance user safety on its current services but also contribute to safety within the metaverse environment.

James Vincent, a senior reporter with eight years of experience covering artificial intelligence (AI), robotics, and related topics at The Verge, reports that the social media conglomerate Meta has become the latest technology firm to develop an "AI supercomputer." This high-performance computing system is specifically engineered to train machine learning models. Meta asserts that its new AI Research SuperCluster (RSC) is currently one of the fastest machines of its kind and, upon completion in mid-2022, is projected to be the fastest in the world. In a statement, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg remarked, “Meta has developed what we believe is the world’s fastest AI supercomputer,” referring to the RSC, which is expected to be finalized later this year. This development underscores the pivotal role of AI research in companies like Meta. Competitors such as Microsoft and Nvidia have previously unveiled their own versions of "AI supercomputers," which differ somewhat from traditional supercomputers. The RSC will facilitate the training of various systems across Meta’s operations, including content moderation algorithms aimed at identifying hate speech on platforms like Facebook and Instagram, as well as augmented reality features anticipated for future hardware offerings. Furthermore, Meta has indicated that the RSC will support the design of experiences within the metaverse, the company's term for an interconnected array of virtual environments, encompassing spaces such as offices and online arenas.

Meta has emphasized that the Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA), which is an ongoing subject of refinement, significantly enhances the company's efficiency regarding performance per watt when executing recommendation workloads. This improvement enables Meta to manage more sophisticated and advanced artificial intelligence (AI) workloads. In the future, it is conceivable that Meta may assign a substantial portion of its AI workloads to arrays of MTIAs. However, at present, the social media platform continues to depend on the graphical processing units (GPUs) housed within its research-oriented supercomputer, the Research SuperCluster (RSC). Initially introduced in January 2022 and developed in collaboration with Penguin Computing, Nvidia, and Pure Storage, the RSC has successfully completed its second-phase construction. According to Meta, the RSC now comprises a total of 2,000 Nvidia DGX A100 systems, which collectively feature 16,000 Nvidia A100 GPUs. The rationale for developing an in-house supercomputer is multifaceted; one contributing factor is the competitive pressure within the industry. For instance, several years ago, Microsoft publicized its AI supercomputer, developed in conjunction with OpenAI, and has recently announced a partnership with Nvidia to construct a new AI supercomputer within the Azure cloud infrastructure. Super Bowl II concluded with the Green Bay Packers emerging victorious over the Oakland Raiders with a score of 33 to 14. In this final game for Packers head coach Vince Lombardi, quarterback Bart Starr distinguished himself by passing for 202 yards and securing one touchdown. In Super Bowl III, the New York Jets defeated the Baltimore Colts with a score of 16 to 7, a game notably characterized by Jets quarterback Joe Namath's pre-game guarantee of victory. Namath fulfilled this promise by throwing for 206 yards, supported by a defensive performance that resulted in three interceptions of Colts quarterback Earl Morrall in the first half.

Super Bowl IV saw the Kansas City Chiefs triumph over the Minnesota Vikings with a score of 23 to 7. Finally, Super Bowl V was decided in the closing seconds, with the Baltimore Colts defeating the Dallas Cowboys 16 to 13, as Colts kicker Jim O'Brien successfully executed a 32-yard field goal. Running back Willie Parker executed a 75-yard touchdown run, contributing to Seattle's defeat, which was exacerbated by penalties, dropped passes, ineffective clock management, and a poorly timed interception by quarterback Matt Hasselbeck. This victory enabled Pittsburgh to equal Dallas and San Francisco for the highest number of Super Bowl wins, with a total of five championships. 

In Super Bowl XLI, the Indianapolis Colts defeated the Chicago Bears 29 to 17 in a match characterized by adverse weather conditions in Miami. Quarterback Peyton Manning spearheaded the Colts' efforts, achieving a long-awaited championship with a performance that included 247 passing yards and one touchdown, thereby facilitating head coach Tony Dungy’s historic achievement as the first African-American coach to secure a Super Bowl title. The game was marked by six fumbles and three interceptions, attributed to the rain-soaked playing field; nonetheless, Manning's triumph was the most significant outcome.

Super Bowl XLII concluded with the New York Giants overcoming the New England Patriots with a score of 17 to 14. The Giants entered the game as 12-point underdogs but had a remarkable streak of ten consecutive road victories, three of which occurred in the playoffs. Meanwhile, the Patriots sought to complete an undefeated season with an 18-0 record, aiming to establish a precedent as the first team to finish a season 19-0. However, the Giants demonstrated considerable resilience through an aggressive pass rush against quarterback Tom Brady, decisive plays by Eli Manning and David Tyree, and an indomitable spirit that the Patriots could not surmount. This contest is frequently regarded as one of the most memorable Super Bowls in history. Additionally, Eli Manning was awarded the MVP title, marking a consecutive achievement for the Manning brothers in securing both championships and Super Bowl MVP honors.

Super Bowl victors are not only designated as the champions of the National Football League (NFL), but they also receive substantial financial rewards through contracts and attain significant celebrity status in the United States following their triumphs. The Super Bowl is an annual American football contest that determines the NFL champion. This event features the winning team from the American Football Conference (AFC) championship game competing against the winning team from the National Football Conference (NFC) championship game. Since the merger agreement between the NFC and AFC in 1967, teams have vied for the Vince Lombardi Trophy, named in honor of the esteemed head coach of the Green Bay Packers. This team was the first to secure victories in the initial two Super Bowls, prompting discussions regarding the competitiveness of the AFC in the event. Initially, the NFL and the American Football League (AFL) were separate entities; however, they merged into a single league in 1970 while subsequently dividing into two conferences, with three NFL teams transitioning to the AFC to achieve parity with 13 teams in each conference. The term "Super Bowl" was coined by Lamar Hunt, the owner of the Kansas City Chiefs, inspired by his daughter’s play with a toy known as the Super Ball. What was intended to be a temporary nickname ultimately became the enduring name for the event. Since its inception in 1967, the AFC has claimed 11 Super Bowl titles, while the NFC has achieved 23 championships. Notably, New Orleans and Miami are favored venues for hosting the Super Bowl, having each hosted the game on nine occasions.

Super Bowl XLIII, which concluded with a score of Pittsburgh 27 and Arizona 23, exemplified a remarkable contest, challenging the notion that the preceding Super Bowl XLII could not be surpassed in terms of drama. For the second consecutive year, the event showcased a thrilling game in which the Arizona Cardinals overcame a 20-7 deficit to briefly seize a 23-20 lead. However, quarterback Ben Roethlisberger responded effectively under pressure, culminating in a remarkable touchdown reception by Santonio Holmes, who was subsequently awarded the Most Valuable Player (MVP) title. Following a period characterized by significant mismatches, the Super Bowl has regained its status as a compelling spectacle worthy of full viewership. 

Super Bowl XLIV, featuring the New Orleans Saints versus the Indianapolis Colts, concluded with a score of New Orleans 31, Indianapolis 17, marking the first encounter between top-seeded teams in the Super Bowl since 1993. Both teams commenced their respective seasons with impressive performances; the Colts achieved a 14-0 record before suffering two consecutive losses, while the Saints began with a 13-0 record before experiencing three successive defeats. Nevertheless, both teams demonstrated dominance throughout the postseason leading up to their meeting in Miami. Although the Colts entered the game as favorites, they had not encountered an offensive unit comparable to that of New Orleans. Despite the Colts establishing an early 10-0 lead, quarterback Drew Brees systematically dismantled the Colts' defense, aided by head coach Sean Payton's strategic decision to execute an onside kick at the beginning of the second half. Ultimately, Tracy Porter's interception return for a touchdown of Peyton Manning in the fourth quarter served as the decisive play. 

Super Bowl LVII concluded with the Kansas City Chiefs defeating the Philadelphia Eagles by a score of 38 to 35.

Atlanta, Miami, Los Angeles, and Tampa Bay were the four candidates for the three Super Bowl contests; Atlanta was awarded Super Bowl LIII, Miami was designated Super Bowl LIV, and Los Angeles, which opted not to bid for Super Bowl LIV and was ineligible for Super Bowl LIII, received Super Bowl LV. On May 18, 2017, officials announced a postponement of the opening of Los Angeles's new stadium, SoFi Stadium, from the beginning of the 2019 season to 2020. Consequently, during the league's owners meetings held in Chicago on May 23, 2017, Super Bowl LV was reallocated to Tampa Bay, the sole remaining candidate, while Super Bowl LVI was awarded to Los Angeles. The official logo for Super Bowl LVI was unveiled on February 9, 2021, adhering to the standard design established since Super Bowl LI, but incorporating sunset-themed elements that reflect the landscape of the host city and its vicinity, specifically featuring palm trees within the traditional Roman numerals. In early January 2022, it was reported that AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas, was under consideration as an alternate venue for the game as a contingency measure due to a rise in COVID-19 infections in California linked to the Omicron variant. This stadium previously hosted Super Bowl XLV in 2011.

Both teams concluded the regular season as the fourth seed in their respective conferences, resulting in this being the first Super Bowl since the implementation of seeding in 1975 that did not feature a top-three seed. The match exhibited three lead changes and predominantly maintained a margin of one possession. At halftime, Los Angeles held a 13–10 advantage; however, the Bengals subsequently scored ten consecutive points during their initial two drives of the third quarter. In the fourth quarter, while trailing 20–16, the Rams achieved a touchdown to reclaim the lead with less than two minutes remaining and successfully halted Cincinnati's final drive on downs. Wide receiver Cooper Kupp, who converted a critical fourth down on the Rams' final offensive possession and scored the decisive touchdown, was awarded the title of Super Bowl MVP. NBC's broadcast of Super Bowl LVI garnered the second-highest viewership in the history of the event, indicating a reversal from several years of declining ratings. The game attracted an average of 112.3 million total viewers across both NBC and its streaming platforms, reflecting an 8% increase in ratings compared to the preceding Super Bowl. Unlike most prior Super Bowls, no bids were accepted for Super Bowl LVI. The bids for Super Bowl LIII, Super Bowl LIV, and Super Bowl LV were all derived from the same pool of candidates during a convening held on May 24, 2016.

query: Who won women's 500m speed skating at the 2022 Winter Olympics?
positive: The women's 500 m competition in speed skating at the 2022 Winter Olympics took place on 13 February at the National Speed Skating Oval, also known as the "Ice Ribbon," located in Beijing. Erin Jackson, representing the United States, secured the Olympic title, marking her inaugural Olympic medal and making history as the first female Black athlete to medal in speed skating. Miho Takagi of Japan achieved the silver medal, while the bronze medal was awarded to Angelina Golikova, competing for the Russian Olympic Committee, who also earned her first Olympic medal. Nao Kodaira, the defending champion and Olympic record holder, was present in the competition. Lee Sang-hwa, the silver medalist from 2018, had retired prior to the event, though she maintained her status as the world record holder during the Olympics. Similarly, the bronze medalist from 2018, Karolína Erbanová, also announced her retirement. Golikova was the 2021 World Single Distances champion in the 500 m category. The silver and bronze medals in that competition were claimed by Femke Kok and Olga Fatkulina, respectively. Prior to the Olympics, Erin Jackson led the 2021–22 ISU Speed Skating World Cup standings in the 500 m distance, having completed eight events, followed by Golikova and Kodaira. Golikova recorded a season-best time of 36.66 seconds in Calgary on 11 December 2021. In the fourth pairing, Takagi emerged as the early leader with a time of 37.12 seconds.

Erin Jackson, the American speed skater currently leading the World Cup standings, is set to make her debut at the 2022 Winter Olympics on Sunday. positive: Jessie Diggins, a cross-country skier trained in Stratton, overcame food poisoning on Sunday to secure her second medal at the 2022 Beijing Winter Games. The 30-year-old athlete, who previously earned a bronze medal in the individual sprint on February 8, achieved a silver medal in the 30-kilometer freestyle race. In an interview with NBC, Diggins expressed the emotional nature of her accomplishment, stating, “That was one of the hardest things I’ve ever done in my whole life, especially because I had food poisoning 30 hours ago, which is why I thought I was going to die at the finish line. My legs were cramping the whole last 17 kilometers. I don’t know how I made it.” With this victory, Diggins, who won a gold medal in the 2018 women’s team sprint, has now attained an Olympic medal in each of the three tiers. She remarked to reporters, “It’s been an emotional roller-coaster, but I am so happy we made it to the end. To have a medal in the sprint and the 30K are the ultimate bookends for me. I have been trying to be a good all-round athlete my whole life, so this has been really cool.” Diggins completed the final cross-country race of the Beijing Olympics in a time of 1 hour, 26 minutes, and 37 seconds, finishing less than two minutes behind Therese Johaug of Norway and 50 seconds ahead of third-place finisher Kerttu Niskanen of Finland.

Jessie Diggins secured a silver medal in the 30km mass start event, thereby becoming the first American cross-country skier to achieve two medals at a single Olympic Winter Games. At the Beijing 2022 Olympics, Diggins, age 30, finished in second place behind Norway's Therese Johaug, who earned her third individual title during these Games, contributing to Norway's overall tally of 25 medals. Previously, Diggins had attained a bronze medal in the individual sprint, marking her as the first American woman to earn a medal in cross-country skiing. This achievement further established her legacy as the preeminent cross-country skier from the United States. Notably, Diggins had previously won the first gold medal for Team USA in this sport alongside Kikkan Randall in the team sprint at the PyeongChang 2018 Olympics. Remarkably, she faced food poisoning on the eve of her final event, which she acknowledged diminished her confidence prior to the race. In a statement to Olympics.com, she remarked, "If I'm being honest, 24 hours ago I felt really bad and I just laid in bed yesterday mostly and ate food and drank sport drinks. But it worked and I think maybe because it's been so cold and so hard, maybe extra rest is what I needed." Given her suboptimal preparation, her immediate collapse upon crossing the finish line was perhaps not unexpected.

Although not every episode is centered on a heist, it is common for the "Leverage" team to engage in attempts—often successful—to appropriate various items, ranging from paintings and critical documents to the reputations of billionaires, thus positioning the series within the heist television genre. The final series under consideration offers a distinctive perspective on the heist genre by focusing on the victims, specifically the hostages involved in a 52-hour bank standoff. This narrative approach prompts inquiry into the robbery itself, encompassing aspects such as its planning, execution, and the motivations that drove the perpetrators to commit the act.

The Kill Point explores the dynamics between Mendez and the hostage negotiator, as each attempts to outmaneuver the other throughout the narrative. Netflix introduces another notable Spanish-language heist series, The Great Heist (El robo del siglo), a six-episode miniseries originating from Colombia. Similar to the previously discussed Hatton Garden, this series is also inspired by actual events. Specifically, it reimagines the historical robbery that occurred in October 1994 at the El Banco de la Republica (Bank of the Republic), where a group of thieves successfully stole approximately $33 million in U.S. currency. This series is particularly noteworthy for its inclusion of character backstories, which provides viewers with insight into the motivations behind the criminal actions. However, this does not imply a sympathetic portrayal of the characters, as they are unequivocally depicted as criminals. A significant narrative development is that they ultimately face capture. Furthermore, the genre is expanded with ITV’s Daylight Robbery, a British crime drama consisting of two series that aired at the turn of the century, featuring a total of eight episodes and focusing on the illicit activities of four seemingly ordinary women.
In May 2022, the television series "The Endgame" was discontinued following the conclusion of its first season. The series is categorized within the genres of crime drama and thriller.
negative: Viewers who appreciate the cat-and-mouse thriller "The Endgame" may find interest in a variety of analogous television programs. The thriller genre is currently experiencing a renaissance, with numerous networks seeking to produce their own interpretations. This sustained popularity can be attributed to the genre's unique capacity to engage audiences, fostering a sense of suspense and curiosity regarding the unfolding narrative and the eventual resolution of its mysteries. "The Endgame," as the latest addition to NBC's lineup, aligns with a substantial array of similar series, both on the network and beyond. For instance, "The Blacklist" offers elements that are likely to resonate with fans of "The Endgame," particularly through the central dynamic between its protagonists, Raymond Reddington and Elizabeth Keene. The series is characterized by its intricate narrative structure, which features multiple enigmas that persist throughout its progression. A significant aspect of its allure can be attributed to James Spader's compelling portrayal of the complex character Reddington, marking one of his most distinguished performances. The figure of the drug lord occupies a prominent place in American popular culture, with few such figures being as infamous as Pablo Escobar.

The narrative of the television series centers on a professional thief named Mickey O’Neil, portrayed by the actor Dougray Scott. O’Neil is depicted as orchestrating one of the most significant heists in Los Angeles, and potentially in the world. He assembles a meticulously selected team of experts to conduct simultaneous robberies of three prominent jewelry stores along Rodeo Drive during the Academy Awards, capitalizing on the distractions presented by the opulence and allure of Hollywood’s elite. 

In a recent addition to the heist television drama genre, NBC has launched a series titled The Endgame. This series features actors Morena Baccarin and Ryan Michelle Bathé, and it follows the character Elena Federova, an international arms dealer and criminal mastermind who has recently been apprehended by the FBI. Federova is known for having orchestrated a series of bank heists throughout New York, the motivations for which remain unclear. The narrative unfolds as FBI agent Val Turner is assigned to the case, presenting the possibility of thwarting Federova’s ambitious plans and uncovering her ultimate objectives.

Moreover, the appeal of observing a heist within a scripted television format is significantly heightened when the narrative is informed by actual events. This is exemplified by ITV’s four-part miniseries titled Hatton Garden, also referred to as The Heist at Hatton Garden, which is based on the real-life burglary of a safety deposit vault located in Hatton Garden, London.

"Avengers: Endgame, released in 2019, is an American superhero film derived from the Marvel Comics superhero team known as the Avengers. The film was produced by Marvel Studios and distributed by Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures, serving as the direct sequel to Avengers: Infinity War, which was released in 2018. Furthermore, it represents the 22nd installment in the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU). The film was directed by Anthony and Joe Russo, with a screenplay authored by Christopher Markus and Stephen McFeely. It features an ensemble cast that includes notable actors such as Robert Downey Jr., Chris Evans, Mark Ruffalo, Chris Hemsworth, Scarlett Johansson, Jeremy Renner, Don Cheadle, Paul Rudd, Brie Larson, Karen Gillan, Danai Gurira, Benedict Wong, Jon Favreau, Bradley Cooper, Gwyneth Paltrow, and Josh Brolin. In the narrative, the remaining members of the Avengers, along with their allies, endeavor to reverse the consequences of Thanos's actions as depicted in Infinity War. Initially announced in October 2014 under the title Avengers: Infinity War – Part 2, this designation was subsequently retracted by Marvel. The Russo brothers were appointed as directors in April 2015, followed by Markus and McFeely's agreement to compose the script a month later. Serving as a culmination of the MCU's overarching narrative up to that juncture, the film concludes the character arcs of several principal figures and revisits key moments from previous installments, thereby reintroducing actors and settings from across the franchise."

On October 30, 2020, it is pertinent to note that for the past decade, Marvel has dominated the adaptation of superhero comic books into cinematic productions. The culmination of this trajectory occurred in 2019 with the release of *Avengers: Endgame*, which achieved significant critical and commercial acclaim. This narrative evolved from a focus on a playboy billionaire, Tony Stark, also known as Iron Man, to an expansive saga encompassing multiple universes and a diverse array of heroes and villains. However, as the narrative universe broadened, the lack of diversity within Marvel's roster of superheroes became increasingly evident, particularly in light of the emergence of other superhero narratives within mainstream media. Concurrently, in the same year that *Endgame* was released, a subversive television series entitled *The Boys* began to gain traction. This series, adapted from a graphic novel of the same name, is recognized for its provocative and often violent content. It explores the implications of superheroes existing within a real-world context, positing that such figures would become commodified entities capable of generating chaos, subsequently managed by their powerful corporate entity, Vought. The narrative examines themes of power, social media influence, and politics, effectively blurring the distinctions between heroism and villainy. Consequently, it is challenging to overlook the parallels between the narratives presented in *The Boys* and the realities depicted within the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU).

The series premiered on February 21, 2022, and was subsequently canceled by NBC on May 12, 2022, after a single season. According to the review aggregator Rotten Tomatoes, the series possesses a 33% approval rating derived from 12 reviews, with an average rating of 5.4 out of 10. The consensus among critics on the website states, "While Morena Baccarin's vampish performance is commendably campy, The Endgame is too contrived and silly to justify its labyrinthine structure." Furthermore, Metacritic reports a score of 44 out of 100 for the series, based on 7 reviews, indicating a status of "mixed or average reviews."

Scott provided a cautiously affirmative critique of the film, observing that "Endgame is a monument to adequacy, a fitting capstone to an enterprise that figured out how to be good enough for enough people enough of the time." He further noted that the narrative is not conclusively over, as Disney and Marvel continue to explore new dimensions within the time-money continuum. Nevertheless, Scott emphasized that the Russo brothers impart a sense of closure, allowing viewers to reflect on the accomplishments prior to the resetting of timelines and the resumption of familiar narratives. In a review for the Los Angeles Times, Justin Chang remarked that "Avengers: Endgame achieves and earns its climactic surge of feeling, even as it falls just short of real catharsis." Several critics have identified the film as a significant enhancement over its predecessor, Avengers: Infinity War. For instance, Brian Tallerico of RogerEbert.com asserted that Endgame is "a more patient, focused film [than Infinity War], even as its plot draws in elements of a dozen other movies." Similarly, Matt Zoller Seitz, also from RogerEbert.com, delivered a favorable evaluation of Endgame in contrast to Infinity War, which he deemed "too crowded, too rushed and yet too long." Seitz characterized Endgame as "a heartfelt and satisfying experience," highlighting its "surprisingly relaxed, character-driven, self-aware yet sincere comedy [for] two-thirds of [the film]."

The screenplay of Avengers: Endgame has drawn comparisons to the relaxed narrative style characteristic of Richard Linklater's films, albeit within the superhero genre. Joe Morgenstern, writing for The Wall Street Journal, recognized the film's significant accomplishment as the culmination of the Infinity Saga, describing the climactic battle as "inevitably unwieldy [...] but thrilling all the same," and noting the subsequent "delicate coda," which encapsulates numerous narratives and adventures that require resolution before the commencement of the next cycle. In contrast, Richard Brody of The New Yorker offered a more critical perspective, suggesting that while the performances were commendable, they were not complemented by equivalent directorial finesse. He remarked, "The Russos have peculiarly little sense of visual pleasure, little sense of beauty, little sense of metaphor, little aptitude for texture or composition; their spectacular conceit is purely one of scale, which is why their finest moments are quiet and dramatic ones." Additionally, Anthony Lane, also from The New Yorker, provided a mixed assessment, describing the film as overdeveloped and overwrought, asserting, "The one thing you do need to know about Avengers: Endgame is that it runs for a little over three hours, and that you can easily duck out during the middle hour, do some shopping, and slip back into your seat for the climax. You won't have missed a thing." Furthermore, at the 92nd Academy Awards, Avengers: Endgame received a nomination for Best Visual Effects.

A soundtrack album featuring Silvestri's score was digitally released by Hollywood Records on April 26, 2019, followed by a physical release on May 24. Silvestri characterized the score as exhibiting the most versatile tone within the franchise, encompassing "thunderous percussion and powerful brass" for the action sequences, alongside minimalist, jazz-inspired compositions for Ant-Man and the Quantum Realm. Engaging narratives in television and film that maintain suspense and unpredictability are often highly valued by audiences. Such productions frequently employ plot twists that subvert initial expectations, leading viewers to experience a continuous sense of anticipation regarding subsequent developments. This phenomenon is particularly pronounced in narratives centered around antiheroes, challenging traditional notions of morality by eliciting empathy for characters typically categorized as antagonists. Notable examples of this genre include "Breaking Bad" and "Money Heist," which contribute distinctively to the discourse surrounding narrative complexity and character development. These series compel audiences to reconsider the dichotomy of heroism and villainy, prompting critical inquiries such as: "Is the antagonist invariably the villain?" This question highlights the nuanced portrayal of characters who, despite their criminal actions, may be motivated by altruistic intentions. Consequently, the exploration of such themes in heist-related television shows invites viewers to reflect on the moral ambiguities inherent in societal perceptions of criminality. Therefore, an examination of these series offers an opportunity for a deeper understanding of the complexities surrounding character morality and narrative structure.

In addition to the portrayal of serial killers and drug lords, American popular culture also engages significantly with the archetype of the fraudster. A contemporary series that exemplifies this theme is *Inventing Anna*, which centers on a young woman who successfully persuaded numerous influential individuals in the United States of her fabricated identity. The series features a prominent intellectual contest between journalist Vivian Kent and the central figure, as Kent diligently seeks to reveal the truth about this extraordinary individual, who presents a varied persona depending on her interlocutor. Furthermore, it became the fifth film to exceed this financial threshold, following "Avatar," "Titanic" (1997), "Star Wars: The Force Awakens" (2015), and "Infinity War." positive: He inaccurately asserted that they had been "facing humiliation and genocide perpetrated by the Kyiv regime." The gameplay encompasses various weapon types and magic spells, incorporating stealth mechanics for non-direct engagement. Checkpoints within the game facilitate fast travel and enable players to enhance their attributes using an in-game currency known as Runes. Elden Ring also features an online multiplayer mode, allowing players to participate in cooperative and player-versus-player combat. Tianwen-1 (TW-1; simplified Chinese: 天问; traditional Chinese: 天問; translated as Heavenly Questions) represents an interplanetary mission executed by the China National Space Administration (CNSA), which dispatched a robotic spacecraft to Mars. This mission comprises six components: an orbiter, two deployable cameras, a lander, a remote camera, and the Zhurong rover. The spacecraft possesses a total mass of nearly five tons, categorizing it among the heaviest probes launched toward Mars, and it is equipped with 14 scientific instruments. Tianwen-1 is the inaugural mission in a series of planned endeavors under the CNSA's Planetary Exploration of China program. The scientific objectives of the mission encompass the investigation of Martian surface geology and internal structure, the search for evidence of both current and past water presence, and the characterization of the Martian space environment and atmosphere. The mission was launched from the Wenchang Spacecraft Launch Site on 23 July 2020, utilizing a Long March 5 heavy-lift launch vehicle. Following a transit duration of seven months through the inner Solar System, the spacecraft successfully entered Martian orbit on 10 February 2021. Subsequently, for a period of three months, the probe conducted studies of the designated landing sites from a reconnaissance orbit.

The Field Test Rover (FTR) was developed two years prior to the construction of the Zhurong rover, with several components of the FTR incorporated into the vehicle dispatched to Mars. Prior to the mission's commencement, the FTR underwent thousands of ground tests. This twin rover continued to operate, assisting scientists and engineers in defining the trajectory for Zhurong by conducting maneuver tests within the Mars yard. The selection of the landing area was based on two primary criteria, leading to the preselection of two potential sites: Chryse Planitia and Utopia Planitia. The candidate site in Utopia Planitia was preferred by the team due to its increased likelihood of providing evidence regarding the historical existence of an ancient ocean in the northern region of Mars. Consequently, it was ultimately designated as the final landing area for the mission. The Tianwen-1 spacecraft, along with the Zhurong rover, was launched at 12:41 UTC+8 on 23 July 2020, from the Wenchang Spacecraft Launch Site using a Long March 5 heavy-lift rocket. Following a journey of 202 days through interplanetary space, Tianwen-1 successfully entered Martian orbit on 10 February 2021, marking China's inaugural Mars orbiter. Subsequently, it executed multiple orbital maneuvers and commenced the survey of target landing sites on Mars in advance of the impending landing attempt.

The Zhurong rover, measuring approximately 1.5 meters in height and possessing a mass of approximately 240 kilograms (530 pounds), was deployed with the accompanying orbiter, which functions as a telecommunications relay while simultaneously conducting its own orbital observations of Mars. On 12 July 2021, the Zhurong rover visited the parachute and backshell remnants that had been deployed onto the Martian surface during its landing on 14 May. By 15 August 2021, Zhurong had successfully completed its scheduled exploration objectives and commenced its traversal towards the southern region of Utopia Planitia, the site of its landing. Subsequently, on 18 August 2021, Zhurong exceeded its operational lifespan of 90 sols, prompting Chinese scientists and engineers to announce an extended mission aimed at exploring an ancient coastal region on Mars. Between mid-September and late October 2021, both the Tianwen-1 orbiter and the Zhurong rover entered a safe mode due to a communications interruption associated with solar conjunction; both instruments resumed active operation following the conclusion of the blackout. On 20 May 2022, Zhurong was transitioned into hibernation mode in anticipation of impending sandstorms and the Martian winter, with programmed instructions to reactivate under suitable temperature and sunlight conditions.

On 11 June 2021, the China National Space Administration (CNSA) disseminated the inaugural set of scientific images captured from the surface of Mars. Additionally, she claimed the conference championship for the Cascade Collegiate Conference (CCC) in the heptathlon, where she set a 30-year conference record with a score of 5,158 points, which ranks among the top ten in the NAIA all-time record book. Olson established three school career records during her championship season, including the highest marks in the women's long jump (5.50m), 100m hurdles (14.31), and heptathlon points. She was recognized as the 2022 CCC Female Track & Field Athlete of the Year and subsequently received the title of 2022 CCC Female Athlete of the Year, becoming the first female in Corban's history to achieve this distinction. Furthermore, Olson's academic performance has consistently ranked among the highest at the University, maintaining a cumulative GPA of 3.

Robert Swoboda, a distance runner for the Corban Warrior Men's Track & Field team, has distinguished himself by receiving the prestigious 2022 Oregon Sports Awards' Small College Male Athlete of the Year honor. This recognition follows an outstanding 2022 season during which Swoboda set school records in the 1500m (3:56.69), 5000m (14:35.75), and 10,000m (29:32.69) events, thereby establishing himself as the long-distance leader for Corban. At the 2022 NAIA Outdoor Track & Field National Championship, he secured the second individual national championship for Warrior Nation that day, achieving this remarkable feat in the 10,000 meters and becoming the first male student-athlete to earn a national title while representing Corban. In addition to this recent accolade, Swoboda has accumulated several other achievements, including the 2022 CCC 5000m championship title, multiple CCC Track Athlete of the Week awards, and qualification for the 2021 NAIA Cross Country National Championships. Coach Bos remarked, "When I first started working with Rob in the fall of 2019, he told me he was going to be a national champion," indicating the athlete's commitment to excellence.

After earning a bachelor's degree in business administration in the spring of 2022 and retaining one additional year of eligibility due to the COVID-19 pandemic, Ridgway rejoined the two distinguished volleyball programs for her final collegiate year to pursue a master's degree in the same field of study.

The name that has been recurrently referenced in various contexts this year is that of Wally and Janie Vohland, former staff and faculty members of Corban University. Janie Vohland commenced her tenure at Corban as a full-time staff member in 1994 and subsequently transitioned into the role of an adjunct instructor within the psychology department. In addition to her teaching responsibilities, she also conducted First Aid/CPR classes until her retirement earlier this year. Throughout her career, Janie served in multiple capacities, including faculty, campus nurse, and Director of Health Services, characterized by her unwavering dedication and compassionate demeanor. Her contributions extended beyond her professional roles, as she fostered a nurturing environment akin to that of a maternal figure for both colleagues and students. Wally Vohland, a graduate of Corban University, held the position of Student Accounts Coordinator for over 25 years, earning a reputation for his caring and devout character among staff and students alike. He was entrusted with the management of Veterans Affairs certifications and 500 Perkins loans. Notably, the Vohlands have demonstrated increased engagement this year by attending every home indoor sports event hosted by Corban, a commitment that has been evident since the mid-1990s. Furthermore, Janie actively participated in informal discussions with students and coworkers, colloquially referred to as "playing Monday Morning Quarterback," in the hallway between classes. Corban's Director of Athletic Communications, Clayton Messerle, remarked, "Despite just finishing my first year as a Warrior, I could instantly tell the love and admiration that the University and its disciples have for Wally and Janie."

The Warrior Canine Connection (WCC) was established in 2003 to address the increasing needs of wounded, injured, and ill veterans who served post-9/11, along with their families and caregivers, by offering complimentary programs and services. The organization delivers free services encompassing mental health, physical health, career counseling, long-term rehabilitative care, and advocacy. The ongoing support provided by WWP to WCC and other nonprofit partners has significantly contributed to the expansion and transformation of critical resources and programs available to America's wounded veterans. During the commencement ceremony, two additional Wag of the Tail Awards for 2022 were conferred; John Shooshan, the founder of The Shooshan Company, which specializes in property management, development, acquisition, and engineering services, was recognized for his contributions to the veteran community. Furthermore, Mission BBQ and its founders, Rosemarie and Bill Kraus, were honored for their substantial support of WCC, which included not only the provision of meals but also their volunteer efforts and leadership contributions to the organization.

The 2022 WWE Hall of Fame constituted a professional wrestling event orchestrated by WWE, marking the induction of the 23rd class into the WWE Hall of Fame. This ceremony occurred on April 1, 2022, at the American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas, coinciding with the evening preceding WrestleMania 38. The event was broadcast live at 10 PM Eastern Time on Peacock within the United States and on the WWE Network in international markets, immediately following the airing of WWE's weekly Friday night program, SmackDown. Notably, the event was highlighted by the induction of The Undertaker into the WWE Hall of Fame. The scheduling of the 2022 WWE Hall of Fame for April 1, 2022, was confirmed in advance, and the announcement of the ceremony's live airing at 10 PM Eastern Time followed the conclusion of SmackDown. On February 18, 2022, The Undertaker was disclosed as the inaugural individual inductee for the WWE Hall of Fame Class of 2022. This announcement elicited widespread acclaim from WWE fans, members of the professional wrestling community, and various media outlets.

In the week subsequent to the widely reported announcement regarding The Undertaker's induction, media outlets noted that numerous WWE fans had expressed support for the proposition that The Undertaker should be the exclusive inductee for the Class of 2022, positing that his legendary status within the organization merited such recognition. This singular honor was also publicly advocated by fellow Hall of Famer Bubba Ray Dudley, Class of 2018, who articulated his belief that such an event would serve as a significant entertainment draw and result in a sold-out arena. The character of "The Undertaker" was conceived by WWE Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Vince McMahon, who made a rare media appearance on The Pat McAfee Show on March 3, during which he announced his intention to induct The Undertaker into the WWE Hall of Fame. In a heartfelt message, McMahon commended The Undertaker both in and out of character, indicating that this induction represented one of the most challenging undertakings of his career due to their longstanding relationship, shared experiences, and close rapport behind the scenes. In response to McMahon's widely publicized announcement, The Undertaker later issued a statement on Twitter, which included the reflection: "After over 30 years of long roads traveled, countless hours of TV, and one hell of a ride together..."

In 2021, Wounded Warrior Project (WWP) was awarded the HIRE Vets Gold Medallion. Subsequently, this year, the Department of Labor established criteria that elevated WWP to the Platinum Medallion status, which includes initiatives such as a pay differential program designed to mitigate income loss during periods of guard or reserve service, a tuition reimbursement program, and a veteran employee retention threshold. In 2020, WWP initiated the Veterans Engagement Team (VET), comprising a diverse assembly of veteran personnel nationwide who provide mutual support to one another and assist newly recruited veterans. This initiative aims to offer guidance, address inquiries, and foster professional development and long-term success for WWP's veteran workforce. Founded in 2003, Wounded Warrior Project began with the fundamental objective of delivering comfort items to injured service members at their hospital bedsides. Over the nearly two decades since its inception, WWP has expanded its offerings to include essential programs and services related to mental and brain health, career counseling, advocacy, physical health and wellness, social connection, and long-term rehabilitative care. Since its establishment, Wounded Warrior Project® (WWP) has consistently addressed the evolving needs of warriors, their families, and caregivers, aiding them in the pursuit of their highest aspirations.

Jeffers Sports Center for each event. Among the numerous noteworthy nominations representing the 2022-23 academic year, none stand out more significantly than the program-defining upset achieved by the Corban Volleyball team during the NAIA National Tournament Semifinals against their longstanding rivals, Eastern Oregon University. After suffering defeats in two of their three encounters with the No. 1-ranked Mountaineers earlier in the season—matches that ultimately influenced the designation of Regular Season Champions and Tournament Champions for the Cascade Collegiate Conference (CCC)—the Warriors re-entered this highly anticipated rematch as underdogs. Having achieved a place in the NAIA semifinals for the first time in the institution's history, the Warriors, ranked No. 4 in the NAIA at that time, delivered a commanding four-set victory over the Mountaineers, effectively eliminating the tournament favorites from contention for the National Championship match.

Patricia Wright, a distinguished anthropologist and primatologist, currently holds the position of Herrnstein Professor of Conservation Biology and Distinguished Service Professor at Stony Brook University. She is also the founder and executive director of the Centre ValBio research station located in Madagascar. Dr. Wright has been awarded the inaugural Conservation Warrior Award, an accolade established by the Seneca Park Zoo in Monroe County, New York, and coordinated by the Seneca Park Zoo Society. This award recognizes individuals whose innovative approaches and dedication to conservation have resulted in significant and enduring impacts on species survival. Furthermore, the award aims to enhance public awareness regarding the commendable efforts conducted globally in collaboration with the Seneca Park Zoo. The award itself is represented by an artistic depiction of a tree emerging from rock, crafted by artists at the Corning Museum of Glass, symbolizing the resilience, strength, and passion that the recipient embodies in the preservation of wildlife and natural habitats. In addition to the physical award, the recipient is granted an unrestricted operating grant of $20,000, generously provided by Claudia and David Weber. On December 7, 2022, Monroe County Executive Adam Bello proclaimed this date as Dr. Patricia Wright Day, urging citizens to actively pursue conservation initiatives both locally and globally, while recognizing and celebrating Dr. Wright's significant contributions to the field. Pamela Reed Sanchez, President and CEO of the Zoo Society, emphasized the necessity of conservation heroes in light of current global challenges as reported in recent news.

In collaboration with her partner, sophomore Megan Dennis, the pair earned several prestigious accolades during the Warriors' NAIA National Runner-Up season, including recognition as members of the NAIA Pacific Northwest First Team, NAIA Pacific Northwest Pair of the Year, NAIA All-Tournament Team, and NAIA National Pair of the Year. Additionally, Ridgway received the NAIA Most Valuable Player award for the 2023 season, marking a significant achievement for the native of McMinnville, Oregon. She is an exceptional individual with considerable talent, and I eagerly anticipate her future accomplishments.”

Sophomore outside hitter Rylee Troutman, recognized as the 2022 CCC Player of the Year and subsequently awarded the NAIA Attacker of the Year, partnered with senior outside hitter Avari Ridgway, the 2021 CCC Player of the Year, to achieve a total of 38 kills at the net and 34 digs in the backcourt. Notably, the Navy and Gold team outperformed the NAIA leaders in blocking for only the second occasion throughout the season, accumulating 12 blocks while Eastern Oregon University (EOU) secured 10, with senior opposite hitter Ally Schmidt contributing to half of the Warriors' blocking efforts. Additionally, service line performance played a crucial role in this significant victory, as Corban registered six aces while effectively limiting EOU to none. Head coach Kim McLain remarked on the experience of the NAIA Championships, emphasizing her team's remarkable performance during the concluding week of November. She stated, "Making it into the final four of the nation out of 226 teams was a feat in and of itself. Getting another opportunity against EOU in the semifinals was a chance to not only make it into the national championship match but also to secure bragging rights within our own conference." When discussing the impact of her student-athletes in that match, McLain noted, "The focus, passion, and most of all unity they played with was second to none."

Michael Schillage, a graduate of St. Michael High School's class of 2006, has been recognized as the 2022 St. Michael High School Alumni of the Year. His contributions to the Warrior community are notable, particularly his dedication to organizing and promoting the Alumni Softball tournament, as well as his active participation in various campus events, including Open House and Career Day. Following his graduation, Schillage pursued higher education at Tulane University, where he majored in Legal Studies in Business and Philosophy. He subsequently attended the Mississippi College School of Law, graduating in 2013. During his legal training, he interned at the East Baton Rouge Parish Attorney’s Office within the Civil Litigation Division, which led to his appointment as a Special Assistant Parish Attorney in the same division. Currently, he operates his own private practice. While attending St. Michael High School, Schillage was actively involved in extracurricular activities, including participation in the football and track and field teams, as well as membership in the ambassadors program, student council, peer ministry, BETA club, and National Honor Society. Schillage recalls fond memories of his high school experience, such as meeting his wife in geometry class, enjoying Mr. Timmerick’s humor, and participating in the Senior Day crawfish boil. He has been married to his high school sweetheart, Julie Marie Barth, also a graduate of the class of 2006, for eight years, and they have two children, Ethan and Savannah, with a third child expected later this year.

The Surface Laptop SE is a laptop computer developed by Microsoft, officially introduced on November 9, 2021. This device serves as an entry-level model within the Surface Laptop series, specifically targeting the educational sector. Notably, the Surface Laptop SE features a plastic chassis and incorporates certain components, such as the keyboard, that are shared with the Surface Laptop Go. Microsoft has indicated that this model was designed with enhanced reparability in mind compared to other Surface models, with replacement parts—including batteries, displays, keyboards, and motherboards—made available through authorized service partners for on-site repair. 

The device is powered by an Intel Celeron CPU, with configurations available that include either the Celeron N4020, accompanied by 4 GB of RAM and 64 GB of internal storage, or the N4120, which features 8 GB of RAM and 128 GB of internal storage. It is equipped with two USB ports, one of which is USB-C. Distinct from other Surface models, the Laptop SE utilizes a round, non-magnetic power connector. Additionally, the device is characterized by a 10.1-inch display with a resolution of 1366×768 pixels and is equipped with a one-megapixel webcam. It is pre-installed with Windows 11 SE, a variant of the operating system designed with specific optimizations for the education market.

The Surface Laptop 3 is available in various configurations, including models featuring a Cobalt Blue exterior with an Alcantara® material palm rest, a Platinum variant with a metal palm rest, and a Matte Black option also equipped with a metal palm rest. Each model is accompanied by a one-year limited hardware warranty. The weight specifications for the Surface Laptop 3 are as follows: the 13.5-inch models in Sandstone and Matte Black weigh 2.84 lb (1,288 g), while the Cobalt Blue and Platinum models weigh 2.79 lb (1,265 g). The 15-inch variants, which include Platinum and Matte Black finishes, have a weight of 3.40 lb (1,542 g). It is important to note that certain software and accessories are sold separately. Additionally, an extended return offer period is available for Surface devices purchased from the Microsoft Store in select markets; the return process must commence within 60 days of receipt by the customer. This offer is not applicable to purchases made by reseller customers, and is limited to a maximum of five device returns per eligible customer, excluding the Surface Hub. The offer may be void where prohibited or restricted by law, and Microsoft reserves the right to modify or discontinue offers at any time. Other exclusions and limitations may apply, and the Microsoft Store return policy governs extended returns. For further information, please refer to the applicable Microsoft Terms of Sale. Regarding battery performance, the Surface Laptop 3 is capable of delivering up to 11.5 hours of battery life, based on typical usage scenarios for Surface devices. Testing was conducted by Microsoft in September 2019 utilizing preproduction software and hardware.

The Surface Laptop SE, codenamed Tenjin, has been introduced by Microsoft as a new, low-cost computing solution specifically designed for the K-8 education sector. Priced at $249, this device is positioned within the competitive landscape of affordable 11-inch laptops aimed at educational institutions. The Surface Laptop SE is characterized by its entry-level specifications and materials, making it Microsoft's most economical Surface device to date. This strategic pricing facilitates bulk procurement by educational establishments, enabling the distribution of devices to classrooms and students in need. The specifications of the Surface Laptop SE include an Intel Celeron N4020 or N4120 processor, with options for 4GB or 8GB of RAM, and storage capacities of either 64GB or 128GB. The device is equipped with an 11.6-inch non-touch display, featuring a resolution of 1366x768, marking a departure from previous Surface models. In terms of connectivity, the Surface Laptop SE offers one USB-A port, one USB-C port, a headphone jack, and a barrel-style AC power port. Availability for orders is currently limited to educational channels, with sales commencing now and extending into 2022.

The Surface Laptop 3 is available in two display sizes, 13.5 inches and 15 inches, and offers configurations with 8GB or 16GB of DDR4 RAM. The processing capabilities include the Quad-core 10th Generation Intel® Core™ i5-1035G7 and the Quad-core 10th Generation Intel® Core™ i7-1065G7 processors for the 13.5-inch model. The 15-inch variant is equipped with either the AMD Ryzen™ 5 3580U Mobile Processor featuring Radeon™ Vega 9 Graphics or the AMD Ryzen™ 7 3780U Mobile Processor with Radeon™ RX Vega 11 Graphics, specifically designed for the Microsoft Surface® Edition. 

Security features include a Firmware Trusted Platform Module (TPM) and enterprise-grade protection through Windows Hello face sign-in. The device operates on Windows 10 Home and comes with a 30-day trial of Microsoft 365.
