positive: The American Oscars telecast omitted significant details regarding the confrontation between Will Smith and Chris Rock. According to a report by the New York Times, Will Smith has received a ten-year ban from the Oscars following his on-stage altercation with Chris Rock, which occurred during a live broadcast of the award ceremony on March 27. This incident transpired in the final hour of an otherwise uneventful telecast. Rock made a humorous remark concerning Jada Pinkett Smith, Smith's wife, prompting Smith to approach the stage and strike Rock. Shortly thereafter, Smith received his first Oscar award. In response to the incident, Diddy expressed surprise, stating, “I did not know this was gonna be the most exciting Oscars ever.” On April 8, the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences issued an open letter, noting, “This action we are taking today in response to Will Smith’s behavior is a step toward a larger goal of protecting the safety of our performers and guests, and restoring trust in the Academy.” The letter further indicated a desire to initiate a process of healing and restoration for all parties involved. Smith, who resigned from the Academy on April 1, acknowledged the Academy's decision in a statement, affirming, “I accept and respect the Academy’s decision.” The context of the incident revealed that Chris Rock was present at the Oscars to announce the winner of the Best Documentary category. Prior to revealing the nominees, he engaged in comedic commentary, which included a focus on Jada Pinkett Smith, who was seated prominently in a green gown and had a shaved head.

The incident commonly referred to as "the slap" represents a significant moment in the history of the Academy Awards. This event occurred during the 2022 Oscars when actor Will Smith struck comedian Chris Rock onstage following a joke made by Rock regarding Smith's wife, Jada Pinkett Smith. In the aftermath of the incident, Smith issued two public apologies and received a ten-year ban from attending Academy events. Nearly a year later, Rock addressed the event in detail during a live comedy special. The 2023 Oscars proceeded without any further incidents of this nature, although the previous year's event remained a topic of discussion during the ceremony. The controversy originated when Rock took the stage to present the Academy Award for Best Documentary Feature. In his introductory remarks, he made a joke referencing a potential sequel to "G.I. Jane," which alluded to Pinkett Smith's shaved head. While Smith initially appeared to find the joke humorous, Pinkett Smith exhibited visible discontent. Subsequently, Smith approached the stage, struck Rock, and returned to his seat, leaving the audience uncertain about the authenticity of the moment. Rock responded to the audience with the remark, "Will Smith just slapped the (expletive) out of me."

On March 27, 2022, during the 94th Academy Awards, actor Will Smith approached the stage and struck comedian Chris Rock in the face while Rock was presenting the award for Best Documentary Feature. This act was a reaction to a joke made by Rock regarding Smith's wife, Jada Pinkett Smith, whose shaved head was a result of alopecia areata, a condition she had been managing since 2021. Following the incident, Smith returned to his seat and directed profane language at Rock, who briefly acknowledged Smith before continuing with his presentation without further disruption. Later that evening, Smith was awarded the Oscar for Best Actor and issued an apology to the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences and his fellow nominees, although he did not extend this apology to Rock during his acceptance speech. The following day, he publicly apologized to Rock and the Academy via social media. On April 1, Smith resigned from his Academy membership in anticipation of possible suspension or expulsion, and he was subsequently prohibited from attending Academy events for a duration of ten years, effective April 8. During the live broadcast of the event, many television networks in the United States muted the incident in compliance with federal censorship regulations. In contrast, uncensored footage from international broadcasts gained significant traction on social media, with an excerpt from the Australian coverage becoming one of the most viewed online videos within the first 24 hours. The incident garnered extensive global attention, effectively overshadowing the remainder of the ceremony. In the aftermath, various publications revisited Rock's previous comments concerning Jada Pinkett Smith, including remarks made in 1997 about her involvement in the Million Woman March during an interview on his late-night talk show, The Chris Rock Show.

Nearly a year subsequent to the incident in which Will Smith struck comedian Chris Rock at the Academy Awards, Rock addressed the event in a stand-up performance broadcast live on Netflix. During this presentation, Rock utilized the concluding moments to deride Smith and his spouse, Jada Pinkett Smith. He remarked, “Now I watch ‘Emancipation,’ just to see him get whooped,” referring to the Civil War-era film in which Smith portrays an enslaved individual subjected to labor for the Confederacy. Following the incident, which garnered widespread attention, Rock, known for his willingness to engage with contentious subjects, initially indicated that he was still processing the occurrence. He assured audiences at his first performance post-Oscars that he would eventually elaborate on the matter. This opportunity arose during a performance at the Hippodrome Theater in Baltimore, which was live-streamed to a broader audience. Throughout his routine, Rock frequently became the subject of his own humor, commenting on Smith's more robust physique. “Will Smith does movies with his shirt off,” Rock noted while pacing the stage in a white ensemble, adding, “You never seen me do a movie with my shirt off.”

During the awards ceremony, Chris Rock addressed Jada Pinkett Smith, stating, “Jada, I love you,” and expressed anticipation for the release of “G.I. Jane 2.” In response, Pinkett Smith exhibited a stony demeanor, prompting Will Smith to rise from his seat, approach the stage, and strike Rock. At this moment, the audio of the United States broadcast was interrupted; however, the event continued to be broadcast uncensored in other countries, including Japan and Australia. Rock reacted by exclaiming, “Wow! Wow!” as Smith returned to his seat, declaring, “Will Smith just smacked the shit out of me, everybody.” Smith then shouted, “Keep my wife’s name out your fucking mouth.” To this, Rock replied, “Wow, dude, it was a G.I. Jane joke.” Smith reiterated his demand, while Rock affirmed his intention to comply, stating, “I’m going to. Okay?” Following a brief pause, the audio in the United States resumed just in time for Rock to remark, “That was the greatest night in the history of television. Okay. Okay.” He subsequently continued with the presentation of the Oscar, which was awarded to “Summer of Soul,” directed by Questlove. Shortly thereafter, Will Smith received the Oscar for Best Actor for his portrayal of Richard Williams, the father of tennis athletes Venus and Serena Williams. In his acceptance speech, Smith remarked, “Richard Williams was a fierce defender of his family” as he took the stage.

The actress from "Girls Trip" hosted a segment featuring guests who discussed the severe repercussions of alopecia, including a mother whose daughter faced bullying due to the condition. The Board of Governors of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has imposed a ten-year prohibition on Smith's attendance at any Academy events, including the Oscars. The organization stated, "The Board has decided, for a period of 10 years from April 8, 2022, Mr. Smith shall not be permitted to attend any Academy events or programs, in person or virtually, including but not limited to the Academy Awards." Smith had previously resigned from the Academy one week before the announcement of the disciplinary actions. At the 2022 Oscars, Will Smith received the award for Best Actor for his role in "King Richard." Following the incident, Rock's mother, Rose, expressed her discontent in an interview with WIS-TV in Columbia, South Carolina, stating, "When he slapped Chris, he slapped all of us. He really slapped me. Because when you hurt my child, you hurt me." Furthermore, Questlove, who won the Academy Award for Best Documentary Feature in the category introduced by Rock, referred to the incident during the 64th Annual Grammy Awards while presenting an award, humorously asserting, "I'm going to present this award and I trust that you people will stay 500 feet away from me. Just playing," to the audience.
On March 27, 2022, at the 94th Academy Awards, actor Will Smith approached the stage and struck comedian Chris Rock in the face during Rock's presentation.

On April 8, 2022, a humorous remark was made by Chris Rock regarding Jada Pinkett Smith, the spouse of Will Smith. In response to this comment, Smith approached the stage and physically assaulted Rock. Shortly thereafter, Smith was awarded his inaugural Academy Award.
negative: Following the event, the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences released a statement asserting its position against any form of violence. The Los Angeles Police Department also acknowledged the occurrence, stating, “The incident involved one individual slapping another.” Furthermore, it was noted that the individual involved opted not to file a police report, though the LAPD indicated its readiness to conduct an investigation should the party decide to pursue a report at a later time. Chris Rock remarked, “That was the greatest night in the history of television,” as he continued his duties as presenter. Shortly thereafter, rapper Sean Combs, who was on stage to introduce a tribute to “The Godfather,” attempted to mediate the situation by suggesting that Will Smith and Chris Rock resolve their differences at an Oscars afterparty, stating, “Will and Chris, we’re going to solve that like family at the Gold party.” The incident prompted numerous individuals to approach Smith and Jada Pinkett Smith during the subsequent commercial breaks; for instance, Keith Urban embraced Smith during one interruption, and Nicole Kidman also approached to exchange a few words. Backstage, during interviews with award recipients, the incident involving Rock and Smith was largely avoided, with Ahmir “Questlove” Thompson, director of the Oscar-winning documentary “Summer of Soul,” stating, “I’m not talking about that.” Ultimately, the confrontation overshadowed Smith’s significant achievement.

The act of Jada Pinkett Smith boycotting the Oscars has been compared to an individual's decision to boycott an event they were not invited to, as articulated by an unnamed source. For further insights regarding the incident involving Will Smith and Chris Rock at the Oscars, one may refer to the daily podcast, PEOPLE Every Day. The incident at the awards ceremony escalated when Rock made a joke about Jada's hair loss, which she has publicly disclosed as a consequence of her prolonged battle with alopecia. Rock stated, "Jada, I love ya. G.I. Jane 2, can't wait to see ya," in reference to her condition. Subsequently, Will Smith, aged 53, approached the stage and struck Rock, resulting in astonishment among the audience and viewers globally. Rock, seemingly taken aback, remarked, "Oh wow," as Smith returned to his seat, to which Rock commented, "Will Smith just smacked the s— out of me." Following this, Smith emphatically instructed Rock to "Keep my wife's name out your f—ing mouth!" After returning to his seat, Smith reiterated his demand. Rock responded, "Wow, dude, it was a G.I. Jane joke," to which Smith insisted once more, "Keep my wife's name out your f—ing mouth!" Rock then replied, "I'm going to, okay?" before declaring, "That was the ... greatest night in the history of television" and proceeded to present the award.

In an interview segment from CBS Mornings, Jim Carrey articulated that an individual does not possess the right to approach a stage and physically assault another person in response to verbal remarks. He further expressed that, if he were in Chris Rock's position, he would have pursued legal action against Will Smith for $200 million, asserting that the recorded incident would persist indefinitely and that the associated insult would endure for an extensive period. Carrey also criticized the ethical standards of the Hollywood film industry, particularly noting the standing ovation awarded to Smith following his acceptance of the Academy Award. Anthony Hopkins, who presented the Academy Award for Best Actress, explicitly commented on Smith's conduct and acceptance speech, remarking, "Will Smith said it all. What more can be said? Let's have peace and love and quiet." Additionally, various comedians, including George Wallace and Kathy Griffin, expressed concerns regarding the implications of the incident for future confrontations, indicating a heightened caution regarding the content of their comedic material. Howie Mandel linked the incidents involving Rock and Dave Chappelle, positing that the physical altercation at the Academy Awards may have initiated a troubling trend, suggesting that "violence triggers violence," and proposing that this could signify a detrimental turning point for the comedic profession.

This document presents a chronological account of the events that have transpired between the two actors following the widely publicized incident at the Oscars. On March 27, 2022, Will Smith approached the stage during the Academy Awards and struck Chris Rock in a live broadcast. This altercation was precipitated by a joke made by Rock concerning Smith's wife, Jada Pinkett Smith, referencing her shaved head, which is a result of her battle with alopecia. Rock stated, “Jada, I love you. G.I. Jane 2, can’t wait to see it,” unaware of the underlying context. Pinkett Smith's reaction was one of disapproval, while Smith initially responded with laughter. Subsequently, Smith confronted Rock on stage and delivered the slap, after which he vociferously admonished Rock from his seat, demanding that he refrain from mentioning his wife. In the subsequent year, Smith has publicly issued multiple apologies, and various individuals who witnessed the incident have shared their perspectives. Additionally, Chris Rock has incorporated references to the incident in his comedic performances. As the 2023 Oscars approach, the following timeline elucidates the developments between Smith and Rock since the occurrence of the slap.

Numerous Internet memes and parodies have emerged from the associated photo and video content. On March 31, additional smartphone footage was released, capturing the perspective of an audience member at the Smiths' table. This footage appears to depict Jada Pinkett Smith's reactions during and after the joke, characterized by visible disapproval—such as rolling her eyes—during the initial comment but subsequently appearing to laugh at Chris Rock's remark, "Will Smith just smacked the shit out of me," and his assertion that it was "the greatest night in the history of television." In a statement issued on March 30, the Academy indicated that Smith had been requested to leave the ceremony, a claim that he reportedly declined. However, others present contest that Smith was not formally asked to exit, whether directly or indirectly through a representative. This led to discord between members of the Academy's leadership and ceremony producer Will Packer regarding the appropriateness of allowing Smith to remain, resulting in no decisive action being taken. Variety noted that Packer "was the key to Smith remaining in his seat." In a subsequent interview with Good Morning America, Packer expressed his opposition to the notion of removing Smith from the venue, citing a belief that Rock would not support such an action. Within a span of forty minutes, Smith was awarded the Best Actor accolade for his portrayal of Richard Williams in the film King Richard.

Rock has not publicly elaborated on the incident, nor did he file a formal police report against Smith. His initial acknowledgment of the slap occurred during a stand-up comedy performance on the Wednesday following the Academy Awards. He remarked that he was “still kind of processing what happened” and stated, “other than the weird thing, life is pretty good.” The event, characterized by chaos, drew considerable public attention. During the stand-up show, Rock refrained from mentioning Smith or Pinkett Smith, asserting his intention not to discuss the awards night: “If you came to hear that, I’m not ... I had like a whole show I wrote before this weekend,” he noted. In contrast, Pinkett Smith addressed the slap during an episode of her talk show "Red Table Talk," wherein she expressed compassion for both Smith and Rock. She articulated her “deepest hope” that “these two intelligent, capable men have an opportunity to heal, talk this out and reconcile,” emphasizing the necessity of mutual support in the current global context. Furthermore, Pinkett Smith remarked, "'Til then, Will and I are continuing to do what we have done for the last 28 years and that's to keep figuring out this thing called life together."

Smith delivered an emotionally charged address centered on themes of divine justification and his obligation to safeguard those in his vicinity. He extended apologies to the Academy, the fellow nominees, and to Venus and Serena Williams, who were present at the event, though he did not explicitly apologize to Chris Rock. Following his remarks, Smith was met with a standing ovation. In the aftermath of the ceremony, award recipients were instructed to limit their responses to inquiries regarding their professional work. Officers from the Los Angeles Police Department engaged with producer Will Packer, who later noted that they were prepared to detain Smith on charges of battery; however, Rock consistently declined to pursue legal action. On May 4, 2022, comedian Dave Chappelle was attacked onstage by an armed audience member during a performance. Rock subsequently joined Chappelle onstage and humorously inquired, "Was that Will Smith?" On April 21, 2022, Netflix canceled a planned sequel to Smith's 2017 urban fantasy action film, Bright, primarily due to the adverse publicity surrounding Smith's altercation. On November 29, 2022, Smith made an appearance on The Daily Show with Trevor Noah, marking his first public interview since the incident. During the discussion of Smith's forthcoming role in the film Emancipation, Noah probed Smith regarding his reflections on the event. Smith characterized the night as "horrific," acknowledging the numerous nuances and complexities involved, and ultimately stated, "I just lost it."

The incident commonly referred to as "the slap" represents one of the most notorious moments in the history of the Academy Awards. This event involved actor Will Smith striking comedian Chris Rock during the 2022 Oscars, following a joke made by Rock regarding Jada Pinkett Smith's shaved head. Later that evening, Smith was awarded the Academy Award for Best Actor for his portrayal of Richard Williams, the father of tennis champions Serena and Venus Williams, in the film "King Richard." Since the occurrence in March, there have been numerous developments, including reactions from various celebrities, responses from Rock and Pinkett Smith, and disciplinary measures faced by Smith from the Academy. Smith has issued public apologies on two occasions, with his most recent and first on-camera apology presented in a YouTube video titled "It's been a minute..." released on July 29. In this video, he addressed frequently asked questions, including the absence of an apology to Rock during his acceptance speech. In reflecting on the incident, Smith stated, "I was fogged out by that point. It's all fuzzy. I've reached out to Chris, and the message that came back is that he's not ready to talk, and when he is, he will reach out. So I will say to you, Chris, I apologize to you."

Following the incident, musician Questlove and film producers David Dinerstein, Robert Fyvolent, and Joseph Patel accepted the Best Documentary Feature award for "Summer of Soul." Observers noted that this accolade may have been overshadowed by the preceding event. The audience, visibly distracted, turned to social media platforms, particularly Twitter, in search of updates regarding Chris Rock and Will Smith. Gabrielle Ulubay of *Marie Claire* remarked that "Summer of Soul" "deserved to have its moment, and Questlove's touching speech and tribute to his parents deserved to have our full attention—but instead, the world kept its mind on Will Smith and Chris Rock and its eyes on Twitter." In a backstage press conference shortly after leaving the stage, Questlove was queried about the incident but chose not to comment, stating, "I'm not talking about that tonight, this is about the Harlem Cultural Festival." During an appearance on *The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon* the following evening, Questlove indicated that he had not perceived the slap at the time, as he was engaged in Transcendental Meditation to alleviate his anxiety prior to the announcement of the winner, and only became aware of the incident "maybe three seconds before I spoke words." Reactions among celebrity attendees at the Academy Awards were mixed, with a predominant condemnation of Smith's violent behavior and support for Rock; however, some individuals defended Smith's actions or refrained from taking a definitive stance on either party.

On July 29, 2022, Smith published a video on YouTube in which he addressed the incident and expressed an apology to Rock, Rock's mother, Rock's brother Tony, Questlove, the other Oscar winners, and his wife Jada, stating that he felt "deeply remorseful" for his actions. The day following the incident, on March 28, the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences (AMPAS) announced the initiation of a formal review. The Board of Governors of the Academy intended to investigate potential actions and consequences in alignment with its Bylaws, Standards of Conduct, and California law, with a meeting scheduled for March 30. AMPAS President David Rubin and CEO Dawn Hudson subsequently communicated with Academy members, indicating that the official review process would require several weeks. On March 29, Smith conducted a six-minute Zoom call with Rubin and Hudson, during which he reiterated his apology. Notably, the Board of Governors was not informed of this conversation while deliberating on disciplinary procedures the following day. A participant in the board meeting remarked that the lack of transparency surrounding the communication was suspicious and indicative of a deficiency in leadership. On March 30, the Academy commenced disciplinary proceedings against Smith for "violations of the Academy's Standards of Conduct, including inappropriate physical contact, abusive or threatening behavior, and compromising the integrity of the Academy." The Academy further stated, "While we would like to clarify that Mr. Smith was asked to leave the ceremony and refused, we also recognize we could have handled the situation differently."

At this juncture in his life, Smith expressed a profound sense of being overwhelmed by what he perceives as divine guidance regarding his role and responsibilities within the world. Throughout his speech, he exhibited visible emotion, addressing various aspects including a defense of his actions, expressions of regret towards the Academy, and an homage to Richard Williams. He articulated, “I want to apologize to the Academy,” and further extended his apologies to his fellow nominees, remarking, “This is a beautiful moment. And I’m not crying for winning an award. It’s not about winning an award for me. It’s about being able to shine a light on all the people.” Smith elaborated on his desire to “lift up” the narratives of individuals such as Richard Williams, who demonstrated protective love for those in his life. Subsequently, on the following Monday, Smith utilized Instagram to convey an apology to Chris Rock, stating, “I would like to publicly apologize to you, Chris. I was out of line and I was wrong. I am embarrassed and my actions were not indicative of the man I want to be. There is no place for violence

In a notable incident during the 94th Academy Awards held on March 27, 2022, Will Smith abruptly rose from his seat and approached the stage to confront comedian Chris Rock. At that moment, Rock had his hands positioned behind his back when Smith struck him across the face with an open hand, resulting in a pronounced sound. Remarkably, Rock maintained his balance following the impact. In response to the altercation, Rock exclaimed, "Oh, wow! Wow! Will Smith just smacked the shit out of me," prompting laughter from the audience, who initially perceived the event as part of a comedic performance. Subsequently, Smith articulated a phrase that is likely to be frequently referenced in the context of the Oscars: "Keep my wife's name out of your fucking mouth." Despite Rock's attempt to clarify that his remarks were intended as humor, Smith reiterated his statement with increased volume and emphasis. This escalation elicited audible gasps and silence from many attendees, while spectators in the mezzanine area stood to observe the unfolding situation. Rock then endeavored to redirect the focus of the event, remarking, "That was the greatest night in the history of television," which garnered a limited response of applause and whistles from the audience.

NBC News acquired a letter signed by David Rubin and Dawn Hudson, which disclosed the Board of Governors' decision, effective on that date, to impose a ten-year ban on Smith, prohibiting him from attending any Academy events or programs, whether in person or virtually, including but not limited to the Academy Awards. In response, Smith provided a brief statement acknowledging, "I accept and respect the Academy's decision." A YouGov opinion poll conducted the day following the ceremony indicated that 61% of Americans deemed Smith's actions unacceptable, while 22% considered them acceptable. The Federal Communications Commission, responsible for regulating U.S. broadcasting, received 66 complaints concerning the incident. SAG-AFTRA, the labor union representing film and television actors, released a statement denouncing Smith's behavior, asserting that "violence or physical abuse in the workplace is never appropriate and the union condemns any such conduct." The union characterized the incident involving Will Smith and Chris Rock during the previous night's Academy Awards as unacceptable and stated that they had been in communication with the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences and ABC regarding the matter, emphasizing their commitment to ensuring that this behavior is appropriately addressed. SAG-AFTRA refrained from commenting on any ongoing disciplinary processes involving its members.

In a notable exchange, Rock remarked, "Wow, dude!" to which Smith responded affirmatively. Rock then referenced his earlier joke about G.I. Jane, prompting Smith to assert, with a rising voice, "Keep my wife's name... out your fucking mouth!" Rock replied, "I'm going to, okay? Oh, I could, oh, okay. That was a... greatest night in the history of television, okay," eliciting laughter from the audience. This interaction led both local attendees and journalists to discern that Smith's reaction was authentic. Kevin Costner, who was positioned offstage to present the Academy Award for Best Director, remarked, "Oh, that was real." Prior to Smith's acceptance speech, the audience—reportedly stunned and bewildered—anticipated an official acknowledgment from the stage; some individuals in the mezzanine even stood to observe the proceedings below. Despite this incident, the show proceeded without hosts and continued as planned. Smith's expletives were muted during the live broadcast in numerous countries. Photo-journalist Brian Snyder, who was working for Reuters in the photographer pool located in the projection booth, captured an image of Smith striking Rock. This photograph rapidly gained traction on social media, featured prominently in news articles, and was transformed into Internet memes. Snyder remained unaware of the cultural significance of the photo until after the conclusion of the ceremony, as his images were automatically transmitted to his editors.

LOS ANGELES, March 27 (Reuters) - In a notable incident during the Oscars ceremony, Best Actor winner Will Smith struck presenter Chris Rock in the face and directed a profane remark at him following a joke concerning his wife's hairstyle. This event, which occurred on Sunday, overshadowed the awards ceremony itself. Approximately 45 minutes later, while accepting his first Oscar for Best Actor for his performance in "King Richard," Smith delivered a tearful apology to the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences and his fellow nominees, though he did not extend an apology to Rock. In his acceptance speech, Smith expressed, "I want to be a vessel for love." This altercation dominated the remainder of the 3.5-hour event and is poised to be remembered as one of the most significant moments in the 94-year history of the Academy Awards. While this incident may adversely affect Smith's reputation within the Hollywood community, it has not resulted in any criminal charges at this time, as the Los Angeles police reported that Rock opted not to file a police report. The altercation was precipitated by Rock's comedic routine, during which he made a jest about Smith's wife, Jada Pinkett Smith, stating, "Jada, I love you. 'G.I. Jane II,' can't wait to see it," referencing the 1997 film "G.I. Jane" in which actress Demi Moore portrayed a character with a shaved head. It remains uncertain whether Rock was aware that Smith's wife is affected by a condition that leads to hair loss.

It is posited that one cannot fully comprehend the challenges faced by others. During the subsequent Academy Awards, host Jimmy Kimmel humorously suggested that, in the event of unforeseen violence, the assailant would be permitted to remain at the ceremony, receive the Best Actor award, and enjoy the afterparty while listening to "Gettin' Jiggy wit It." Following the ceremony, a sign parodying workplace "days without incident" featured a counter indicating one incident. At the conclusion of his Netflix special, Selective Outrage, comedian Chris Rock made several jokes pertaining to the incident and Will Smith. In response to public backlash, Smith issued a formal apology via Instagram and Facebook, characterizing his conduct as "unacceptable" and "inexcusable." He directly addressed Rock, stating: "I would like to publicly apologize to you, Chris. I was out of line and I was wrong. I am embarrassed, and my actions were not indicative of the man I want to be. There is no place for violence in a world of love and kindness." Three days post-incident, Rock briefly referenced the matter for the first time during a comedy show, indicating that he was still processing the events but would address it more thoroughly in the future. He noted that Smith had not reached out to him personally, nor had they engaged in any conversation since the ceremony. Assertions circulating on social media claiming that Rock had apologized were subsequently discredited by fact-checkers.

In a recent interview with the Los Angeles Times, Kenny expressed his belief that Smith's apology was merely a public relations strategy. He stated, "No, I don’t accept it because I don’t think it was genuine," suggesting that Smith's publicist and his associates likely advised him to issue the apology. Additionally, another of Rock's brothers, Tony, voiced his rejection of Smith's apology, characterizing the entire incident as "foul." Kenny further noted, "I might have looked at it differently had he initially apologized when he got on the stage and cried and accepted the award, but he didn’t, so, right there that tells me that it is something else." Regarding Smith's decision to remain at the ceremony after the altercation with Rock and subsequently accept the Best Actor award for his performance in King Richard, Kenny, along with other attending celebrities, criticized this as a misstep on the part of the Academy. He remarked, "He should have been escorted out of there. I hold them accountable for that. He could have gone up there and done anything you wanted to my brother. It could have been much worse than what he did." Furthermore, Kenny asserted that Smith "belittled my brother. He had no respect for him... In my opinion, he embarrassed himself by doing that and his legacy by doing that."

Noah highlighted a significant aspect of Smith’s memoir, wherein the actor disclosed his childhood fear of conflict. In his own words, Smith reflected, “It was a lot of things. It was the little boy that watched his father beat up his mother, you know. All of that just bubbled up in that moment. That’s not who I want to be.” The interview can be accessed here. Additionally, Academy President Janet Yang emphasized during the 2023 Oscars Nominees Luncheon that the organization’s response to the events of the 2022 ceremony was “inadequate” and asserted the necessity for “swift, compassionate and decisive” action regarding similar issues in the future. Yang remarked, “As I’m sure you all remember, we experienced an unprecedented event at the Oscars. What happened on stage was wholly unacceptable, and the response from the organization was inadequate. We learned from this that the Academy must be fully transparent and accountable in our actions, particularly in times of crisis.” She further stated, “We must act swiftly, compassionately, and decisively for ourselves and for our industry. You should and can expect no less from us going forward. We are committed to maintaining the highest of standards while creating the changes we wish to see in our industry.” This discussion occurred during Netflix’s inaugural live special, Chris Rock: Selective Outrage.

Smith subsequently issued an apology, albeit without referencing Rock. He stated, “I want to apologize to the Academy, I want to apologize to my fellow nominees,” while becoming visibly emotional. “This is a beautiful moment and I’m not crying for winning an award. It’s not about winning an award for me. It’s about being able to shine a light on all of the people … Art imitates life. I look like the crazy father just like they said … just like they said about Richard Williams. Love will make you do crazy things.” Smith was awarded Best Actor in a Leading Role for his remarkable performance in 'King Richard.' Congratulations! #Oscars. The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) stated in a press release, as reported by NBC, “LAPD investigative entities are aware of an incident between two individuals during the Academy Awards program. The incident involved one individual slapping another. The individual involved has declined to file a police report. If the involved party desires a police report at a later date, LAPD will be available to complete an investigative report.” Furthermore, NBC News indicated that representatives for Rock did not respond to requests for comment, and senior sources within the LAPD noted that officers typically refrain from taking action in such suspected misdemeanor battery investigations unless the victim expresses a desire to press charges or assist in completing an incident report. The Academy remarked via Twitter, “The Academy does not condone violence of any form.”

Comedic actor Cedric the Entertainer expressed at an Oscars after-party that he would take measures to defend himself if he were to be assaulted during a stand-up performance for making jokes about an individual's spouse. Comedian James Corden praised Rock for maintaining the flow of the event and criticized Smith for his inability to accept humor; he parodied the incident on The Late Late Show with James Corden by performing a rendition of "We Don't Talk About Bruno" that referenced Pinkett Smith. Amy Schumer articulated that she experienced trauma as a result of the incident and criticized Smith, referring to him as "Ali." She also questioned the degree of control exerted by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences (AMPAS) over the ceremony, noting her restriction from joking about Halyna Hutchins's death in the Rust shooting incident in contrast to Smith's apparent freedom to physically assault another individual. Comedian Gilbert Gottfried's final social media post prior to his death on April 12 expressed support for Rock, posing the question, "Which is the worst crime? Chris Rock being physically assaulted or Chris Rock telling a joke?" The novel presents a compelling account of a scientist navigating the challenges of outer space, alongside another scientist who observes his journey, as well as the family members left behind, highlighting the extreme measures individuals will undertake to safeguard their loved ones and the environment.

For two decades, the protagonist, Alex, has maintained a belief that his genetically modified superalgae can mitigate and potentially reverse the consequences of climate change. His unwavering focus on his research has adversely affected his marital relationship, his connections with his children, and his professional trajectory. When the Son sisters, founders of the prominent technology firm Sensus, present him with an opportunity to finalize his research, he readily accepts. However, this opportunity entails conducting his work in outer space at Parallaxis, the inaugural luxury residential space station designed for affluent individuals. Consequently, Alex and six other scientists depart from Earth and their families to become Pioneers, the initial occupants of Parallaxis. Nevertheless, they soon discover that Parallaxis does not conform to the grandiose expectations that were initially promised.

By Sandra Newman A HOUSE BETWEEN THE EARTH AND THE MOON By Rebecca Scherm Rebecca Scherm’s second novel, “A House Between the Earth and the Moon,” explores the concept of Parallaxis, a space station conceived by the technology corporation Sensus as an orbital luxury condominium for the wealthy elite. Sensus has assembled a team of leading scientists tasked with the construction of the station and ensuring its long-term habitability. In exchange for their expertise, these scientists and their families are promised residence in the station, thereby securing their safety from the catastrophic climate change that threatens society below. However, they are not informed that they will also serve as experimental subjects; Sensus is utilizing the station to trial Views, its new, classified surveillance and behavioral modification system. The narrative evokes a profound sense of anxiety regarding a 2030s Earth, where extreme weather events have become so prevalent that entire cities are frequently engulfed in flames, leading even the affluent to adopt nomadic lifestyles in order to evade impending natural disasters. Widespread fatalities are a regular feature of daily news reports, and the concept of privacy has become obsolete, accessible only through disengagement from societal systems that would otherwise provide evacuation alerts in the event of approaching floods or wildfires. The protagonist, Alex, embodies a sense of urgency in his efforts to develop carbon-extracting algae, which has evolved into a race against time: “He wanted to save his planet, and with each disastrous year, his work became more necessary and less possible."

The portrayal of the Son sisters as individuals confronting commonplace challenges is striking, particularly in the context of their engagement with Views, a mass mind-control initiative. The suggestion to hold the tournament in November elicited controversy, particularly due to its potential impact on the regular season schedules of various domestic leagues globally. Argentina emerged as the champions, defeating the reigning titleholder France 4–2 in a penalty shootout after a 3–3 draw following extra time. On December 19, 2022, Argentina was declared World Champions following their victory over France in a penalty shootout during the final match. Additionally, on December 10, Morocco secured a 1-0 victory against Portugal, while England was defeated by France with a score of 1-2. On December 13, Argentina triumphed over Croatia with a score of 3-0, and on December 14, France won against Morocco, concluding the match at 2-0. On December 17, Croatia achieved a 2-1 victory over Morocco. The final match on December 18 ended in a 2-2 draw between Argentina and France, with Argentina prevailing 4-2 in the penalty shootout. The tournament also included discussions regarding the qualification status for the Qatar 2022 World Cup, including the current state of play and qualification tables.

On December 3-6, the World Cup round of 16 matches were conducted, resulting in the following outcomes: the United States defeated Argentina with a score of 2-1, Australia lost to France 3-1 against Poland, and England triumphed over Senegal with a score of 3-0. On December 5, Japan and Croatia ended their match in a 1-1 draw, with Croatia proceeding to victory through penalties, winning 3-1. Brazil secured a 4-1 victory against South Korea on December 6, while Morocco and Spain concluded their match in a goalless draw, with Morocco winning 3-0 on penalties. Portugal achieved a decisive victory over Switzerland, scoring 6-1 in the quarter-finals on December 9.

Subsequently, on December 10, Croatia faced Brazil, resulting in a 1-1 stalemate, with Croatia advancing 4-2 on penalties. On the same day, the Netherlands drew with Argentina 2-2, but Argentina secured their advancement with a 4-3 win in the penalty shootout. In the semi-finals held on December 13, Morocco lost to England 1-2, while Argentina achieved a 3-0 victory over Croatia on December 14. France won against Morocco with a score of 2-0 on December 17.

The final match occurred on December 18, featuring Argentina and France, both of which had previously secured the championship title on two occasions. Argentina commenced the match with an advantage, facilitated by early goals from Lionel Messi and Ángel Di María, establishing a 2–0 lead against their French counterparts. Despite implementing several substitutions in the first half, France did not manage a shot on goal until after the 70th minute; however, they were invigorated by additional substitutions made in the 71st minute. Shortly thereafter, a penalty was awarded to France when Randal Kolo Muani was fouled in the penalty area by Nicolás Otamendi. Kylian Mbappé successfully converted the penalty and subsequently netted a second goal less than two minutes later, thereby equalizing the match. With the score level at two goals each, the contest proceeded to extra time. Messi scored a second goal in the 108th minute, restoring Argentina's lead. Nevertheless, Mbappé was granted a second penalty in the 115th minute following a handball by Gonzalo Montiel. Mbappé completed his hat-trick, becoming only the second player in history to achieve this feat in a World Cup final, following Geoff Hurst of England in 1966. With the match concluding at a 3–3 tie, the outcome was ultimately decided through a penalty shootout.

The semi-finals occurred on December 13 and 14. In one match, Lionel Messi scored a penalty, followed by Julián Álvarez, who netted two goals, leading Argentina to a 3–0 victory over Croatia. Joško Gvardiol scored first for Croatia, but Achraf Dari quickly equalized just two minutes later. Ultimately, Mislav Oršić netted the decisive goal for Croatia, concluding the match at 2–1.

Additionally, on 11 December 2022, the match ball for the semi-finals, third place match, and final of the 2022 FIFA World Cup was unveiled. This ball, a variant of the Adidas Al Rihla utilized throughout the tournament, was named the Adidas Al-Hilm, which translates to "The Dream" in Arabic, symbolizing the aspiration of every nation to claim the FIFA World Cup title. Although the technical specifications of the ball remained consistent with those of the Al Rihla employed during the group stages and earlier knockout rounds, its color scheme differed, featuring a design in Gold Metallic, maroon, Collegiate Burgundy, and red—colors that reference the national identity of the host nation, Qatar, along with the golden hues associated with Lusail Stadium, the site of the final, and the FIFA World Cup Trophy itself.

The fifth special ball designated for FIFA World Cup final matches, subsequent to the +Teamgeist Berlin, Jo'bulani, Brazuca Final Rio, and Telstar Mechta, was utilized for the event. This venue was earmarked to host the final as part of Qatar's bid for the World Cup and was officially confirmed as the final location on 15 July 2020. Lusail Stadium was also designated to accommodate nine additional World Cup matches, comprising six group stage matches and three knockout phase fixtures. Owned by the Qatar Football Association, the stadium was constructed as part of Qatar's successful World Cup bid. The design of the stadium was carried out by the British firm Foster and Partners in collaboration with Populous, with additional support from MANICA Architecture. The stadium employs solar power for cooling and is reported to have a carbon-neutral footprint. Construction activities commenced in April 2017, with an initial completion target set for 2020. However, the completion of the stadium was delayed, and construction was ultimately finalized in November 2021. The inaugural match at the stadium, the Lusail Super Cup, took place on 9 September 2022, later than initially anticipated.

Due to outbreaks of COVID-19 within their squads, both Vanuatu and the Cook Islands withdrew from participation owing to travel restrictions. Of the 32 nations that qualified for the 2022 FIFA World Cup, 24 had competed in the previous tournament held in 2018. Qatar was the sole team making its debut in the FIFA World Cup, thereby becoming the first host nation to debut in the tournament since Italy in 1934. Consequently, the 2022 tournament marked the first World Cup in which none of the teams that qualified were making their inaugural appearance. The Netherlands, Ecuador, Ghana, Cameroon, and the United States returned to the tournament after absences in 2018, while Canada made a return after a 36-year hiatus, having previously participated in 1986. Wales achieved their first appearance in 64 years, which represents the longest gap for any team, with their sole prior participation occurring in 1958. Italy, a four-time champion and the reigning European champions, experienced a historic failure to qualify for a second consecutive World Cup, having lost in the qualification play-off semi-finals. Notably, the Italians were the only former champions and the highest-ranked team in the FIFA Men's World Rankings that did not secure qualification.

In August 2022, the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA) expanded the final squad size from 23 to 26 players, a modification from the previous tournament held in 2018. All participating teams submitted final squads consisting of 26 players, with the exceptions of France, which opted not to replace the injured Karim Benzema, and Iran, which finalized its squad with 25 players. The final draw for the tournament occurred at the Doha Exhibition and Convention Center in Doha, Qatar, on April 1, 2022, at 19:00 Arabia Standard Time (AST), prior to the completion of the qualification rounds. At the time of the draw, the identities of the two winners of the inter-confederation play-offs, as well as the winner from Path A of the UEFA play-offs, had yet to be determined. The event was attended by approximately 2,000 guests and was conducted by Carli Lloyd, Jermaine Jenas, and sports broadcaster Samantha Johnson, with assistance from notable figures such as Cafu (Brazil), Lothar Matthäus (Germany), Adel Ahmed Malalla (Qatar), Ali Daei (Iran), Bora Milutinović (Serbia/Mexico), Jay-Jay Okocha (Nigeria), Rabah Madjer (Algeria), and Tim Cahill (Australia). For the purposes of the draw, 32 teams were categorized into four pots based on the FIFA Men's World Rankings as of March 31, 2022.

France's Stéphanie Frappart, Salima Mukansanga from Rwanda, and Yoshimi Yamashita from Japan made history as the first female referees appointed to officiate a men's World Cup. Frappart had previously presided over the 2019 FIFA Women's World Cup Final. They were accompanied by three female assistant referees: Neuza Back, Kathryn Nesbitt, and Karen Díaz Medina. On 1 December, Frappart became the inaugural female referee to oversee a World Cup match, officiating the Group E encounter between Costa Rica and Germany. Gambian referee Bakary Gassama and Argentine assistant referee Juan Pablo Belatti were among those serving at their third World Cup, with Belatti having been an assistant referee in the 2018 final. Other returning officials included referees César Arturo Ramos from Mexico and Janny Sikazwe from Zambia, as well as Iranian assistant referee Mohammadreza Mansouri. On 15 December 2022, FIFA announced that Polish referee Szymon Marciniak would officiate the final. The tournament's opening ceremony occurred on Sunday, 20 November 2022, at the Al Bayt Stadium in Al Khor, preceding the inaugural match between the host nation Qatar and Ecuador.

This ball represents the fifth special edition created for FIFA World Cup final matches, following the +Teamgeist Berlin (2006), Jo'bulani (2010), Brazuca Final Rio (2014), and Telstar Mechta (2018).

The bidding process for hosting the 2018 and 2022 FIFA World Cups commenced in January 2009, with national associations required to express their interest by 2 February 2009. Initially, there were 11 bids for the 2018 FIFA World Cup; however, Mexico later withdrew from the bidding process, and Indonesia's proposal was rejected by FIFA in February 2010 due to the Indonesian Football Association's failure to provide a letter of government guarantee to support their bid. The remaining bids for the 2022 FIFA World Cup were submitted by Australia, Japan, Qatar, South Korea, and the United States. Pot 1 comprised the host nation, Qatar, which was automatically assigned to position A1, along with the seven highest-ranked teams. Pot 2 included the subsequent eight highest-ranked teams, while Pot 3 contained the next eight teams in the ranking. Pot 4 was designated for the five lowest-ranked teams, in addition to placeholders for the two winners of inter-confederation play-offs and the winner of the UEFA Path A play-off. A restriction was imposed whereby teams from the same confederation could not be drawn into the same group, with the exception of UEFA teams, for which a maximum of two teams per group was permitted. This regulation also extended to the placeholder teams, with specific constraints determined by the confederation affiliations of both potential winners of each play-off match. The draw commenced with Pot 1 and concluded with Pot 4, with each selected team being allocated to the first available group in alphabetical order. The position of each team within the group was subsequently drawn for the purpose of scheduling matches, with teams from Pot 1 automatically placed in position 1 of each group. For the first time in the history of the FIFA World Cup, an official soundtrack comprising multiple songs has been released, as opposed to a single official song. The album's inaugural track, "Hayya Hayya (Better Together)," features performances by Trinidad Cardona, Davido, and AISHA, and was released on April 1, 2022, alongside its music video. The subsequent track, "Arhbo," performed by Gims and Ozuna, was released on August 19, 2022, with its accompanying music video. The third track, "The World Is Yours to Take," features American rapper Lil Baby in collaboration with Budweiser and was released on September 23, 2022, along with its music video. The fourth track, "Light The Sky," is performed by Nora Fatehi, Manal, Rahma Riad, and Balqees, with composition by RedOne, and was released on October 7, 2022, together with the music video. A fifth track, "Tukoh Taka," featuring performances by Nicki Minaj, Maluma, and Myriam Fares, was released on November 17, 2022, along with its music video, and serves as the official song for the FIFA Fan Festival. The final track, "Dreamers," performed by Jungkook of BTS, was released on November 20, 2022, and was performed with Fahad Al-Kubaisi during the opening ceremony of the tournament.

On April 10, Musk announced his decision not to join the Twitter board. In a statement regarding Musk's choice, Agrawal articulated, "There will be distractions ahead but our goals and priorities remain unchanged. The decisions we make and how we make them remain in our hands, no one else's." Subsequently, on April 14, Musk proposed to acquire Twitter at a price of $54.20 per share, which would value the company at approximately $43 billion, as indicated in a securities filing. This offer represents a 38% premium over the stock price from the day preceding the public disclosure of Musk's investment in Twitter. In this filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission, Musk expressed, "I invested in Twitter as I believe in its potential to be the platform for free speech around the globe, and I believe free speech is a societal imperative for a functioning democracy. Twitter needs to be transformed as a private company." On April 15, Twitter implemented a poison pill provision to obstruct Musk's acquisition attempt. This provision permits existing shareholders to acquire additional shares at a discounted rate, thereby diluting Musk's ownership stake and increasing the financial burden of acquiring the company. Twitter announced that the poison pill would be activated if any individual or entity secures at least 15% of the company's shares. By April 21, Musk reported in a securities filing that he had obtained commitments totaling approximately $46.5 billion in financing for a potential acquisition of Twitter.

In a TED interview, Musk articulated his intention to transform Twitter into a "platform for free speech around the globe," emphasizing that free speech constitutes a "societal imperative for a functioning democracy." He maintained that his proposal was not motivated by personal financial gain. Critics have observed that Musk appeared more focused on modifying Twitter's content moderation policies than on addressing issues of government censorship. As reported by The Washington Post, the suspension of accounts such as The Babylon Bee served as a catalyst for Musk's decision to pursue the acquisition. The proposed acquisition price of $54.20 per share is widely interpreted as an allusion to the number 420, which is commonly associated with cannabis culture. On April 15, Twitter's board of directors announced a "poison pill" strategy, enabling shareholders to purchase additional shares in the event of a hostile takeover, with the plan set to expire on April 14, 2023. Subsequently, on April 17, Twitter's largest institutional shareholders encouraged the company's leadership to "seriously consider" Musk's offer. On April 20, Musk revealed that he had secured financing from a consortium of banks, including Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, Barclays, MUFG, Société Générale, Mizuho Bank, and BNP Paribas, to facilitate a potential tender offer for the acquisition of the company.

In response to a Twitter thread dated May 16, in which Agrawal characterized an external review of the platform's user base as impractical, Musk utilized a fecal emoji in his tweet. The subsequent day, Musk reiterated that the acquisition could not proceed until Twitter was able to substantiate the validity of the aforementioned reports, simultaneously urging the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to investigate Twitter's reported daily user metrics. On the same date, Twitter submitted additional documentation to the SEC, which included a comprehensive timeline regarding Musk's acquisition, and asserted its intention to "enforce the merger agreement" irrespective of Musk's actions. On May 25, Musk revised his financing strategy, abandoning plans to partially fund the acquisition through margin loans secured by Tesla stock, and instead committed to an additional $6.25 billion in equity financing. On that same day, Dorsey resigned from Twitter's board, while Twitter investor William Heresniak initiated a class-action lawsuit against Musk, alleging violations of California corporate laws through market manipulation. The lawsuit further contended that Musk was not authorized by the acquisition agreement to suspend the transaction, and it asserted that Musk's misleading statements had adversely affected Twitter's stock prices. On June 3, the acquisition was approved following a review by U.S. antitrust authorities. Three days later, an email from Musk's attorney to Twitter indicated Musk's intention to terminate the agreement, citing the company's refusal to provide user-related data.

Agrawal, Segal, and Gadde were scheduled to receive "golden parachute" sums of $38.7 million, $25.4 million, and $12.5 million, respectively. However, The New York Times reported that Musk was unlikely to fulfill these payments due to the executives' dismissal for cause. The Financial Times indicated that Musk justified this position by asserting that the company had been mismanaged, and the executives were "weighing their legal options over the decision." Dorsey retained his ownership stake valued at $1 billion, while several other executives left Twitter in the subsequent days. Musk assumed the role of CEO, consolidating the company with X Holdings and dissolving Twitter's board of directors. This merger resulted in Twitter no longer functioning as an independent entity, with X Corp. subsequently established in March 2023 to encompass the company. Musk refers to his position as CEO by the title "Chief Twit." A "war room" was created at Twitter, where Musk convened with Spiro, Sacks, and others to deliberate on the next steps. According to The New York Times, the group’s primary objectives were to reduce the size of Twitter's workforce and to revamp the platform's mobile application.

In a video conference with financial institutions that facilitated Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter, Musk assured the participants of his commitment to completing the buyout within the stipulated timeline. On October 26, Musk visited Twitter's headquarters, during which he tweeted a video depicting himself carrying a kitchen sink in the lobby and modified his Twitter biography to read "Chief Twit." He also communicated to Twitter employees that while layoffs were probable, he did not intend to implement them at the scale previously reported by the media. The following day, Musk composed an open letter to advertisers, asserting that Twitter would not devolve into a "free-for-all hellscape." He emphasized that his motivations for the acquisition were not driven by greed, but rather by an aspiration to establish "a common digital town square." Furthermore, he requested that engineers from Tesla convene with Twitter's product managers to evaluate the platform's codebase, which was temporarily suspended until November 1. On the afternoon of October 27, Musk finalized the acquisition, subsequently announcing via Twitter that "the bird is freed." Following this transaction, Musk assumed ownership of Twitter and immediately dismissed several executives, including CEO Parag Agrawal, Chief Financial Officer Ned Segal, Chief Legal Officer Vijaya Gadde, and General Counsel Sean Edgett, all of whom were escorted from the premises by security personnel.

Following the acquisition of a stake in Twitter at the beginning of the year, Mr. Musk subsequently presented a $44 billion offer in April, a valuation that appeared excessive almost immediately upon agreement. He articulated his rationale for the purchase as a desire for "civilisation to have a common digital town square," asserting intentions to eliminate spam accounts and uphold the platform as a space for free expression. However, by the summer, he revised his position regarding the acquisition, citing apprehensions that the prevalence of fake accounts on the platform exceeded Twitter's assertions. In response, Twitter executives initiated legal proceedings to compel Mr. Musk to fulfill his offer. Although he recently acknowledged that he and the other investors involved in the transaction were "overpaying for Twitter right now," the acquisition appears to have been completed. Nonetheless, speculation persists concerning Mr. Musk's objectives for the platform. Earlier in the week, he modified his Twitter profile to designate himself as "Chief Twit," leading some analysts to surmise that he may assume direct control of the company. Additionally, Mr. Musk shared a video of himself entering Twitter's headquarters in San Francisco while carrying a sink, accompanied by the caption: "let that sink in!" As of now, there has been no official commentary from Twitter regarding its new management structure.

Publicly, he initiated an inquiry into the actual number of authentic accounts on Twitter. The billionaire, identified by Forbes and Bloomberg as the world's wealthiest individual with an estimated net worth of approximately $250 billion (£216 billion), had expressed concerns regarding the prevalence of bots on the platform for several years. Following the acceptance of his acquisition offer, he persistently requested that Twitter furnish data concerning its genuine user base. In response, Twitter executives disclosed their estimation that less than 5% of daily active users, derived from a random sampling of accounts, were classified as bots. This revelation appeared to provoke significant dissatisfaction from Mr. Musk. Subsequently, following an extensive Twitter thread from Mr. Agrawal elucidating the methodology behind this figure, Mr. Musk's reaction was limited to the use of the poop emoji. As the situation deteriorated, it became evident that the agreement was unraveling. On July 8, Mr. Musk announced his intention to withdraw from the transaction. The motivations behind his decision—whether to negotiate a more favorable price or to terminate the agreement—remained ambiguous. Twitter, however, rejected this withdrawal, asserting that Mr. Musk's agreement to acquire the company constituted a legally binding contract and that retracting the deal was not feasible. With substantial legal representation engaged by both parties, a court date was scheduled in Delaware for October 17 to determine whether Mr. Musk would be compelled to proceed with the acquisition. In court filings, Twitter contended that it had provided Mr. Musk with sufficient information regarding the number of authentic users on its platform.

The mechanisms by which his acquisition of Twitter would enhance his capacity to disseminate messages or construct narratives beneficial to Tesla and his economic interests remain unclear. However, it is equally uncertain that such an enhancement would not occur. Given his intellectual capabilities, one plausible narrative posits that Twitter's revenue generation is relatively modest when compared to that of Facebook and other social media entities. Furthermore, the company's market capitalization is not particularly substantial. He could argue, "I derive considerable value from this direct access to the public." The ownership of this direct public engagement platform—an asset that generates significant value for both Elon Musk and Tesla—must possess inherent worth, whether through augmenting the value it produces for Tesla or by identifying methods to monetize the value it generates for sports figures, celebrities, and prominent political figures, such as Donald Trump. For instance, Trump's tweets during his tenure on Twitter had the capacity to influence market dynamics to the tune of billions or even trillions of dollars, yet Twitter itself did not capitalize extensively on such activities. While it is not immediately apparent how the company could monetize this potential, it stands to reason that an individual in a position of intellectual acumen could devise strategies to extract value from such a high-potential asset.

Elon Musk required an additional $22.4 billion to fulfill the equity financing component of the acquisition. Following the disclosure of co-backers, Apollo Global Management Inc. and Sixth Street Partners retracted their offers. Musk, aged 51, holds the title of the world's wealthiest individual, with a net worth estimated at $222 billion by Forbes; however, a significant portion of his wealth is linked to his investments in Tesla and SpaceX. A calculation by Reuters indicated that Musk possessed approximately $20 billion in liquid assets after divesting part of his Tesla holdings through various transactions in November and December of the previous year, as well as in April and August. To finalize the financing for the acquisition, Musk would have needed to secure an additional $2 billion to $3 billion. The method by which Musk addressed a financing shortfall of roughly $3 billion remains unclear. Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives suggested that external capital might have been utilized, as no Form 4s were submitted during the week, indicating the possibility of involvement from an existing investor in the deal. It was anticipated that Musk would liquidate further Tesla shares during the nine-day interval between the electric vehicle manufacturer's earnings report on October 19 and the October 28 deadline for concluding the acquisition.

In a recent discourse, Amazon founder Jeff Bezos raised inquiries regarding the implications of Tesla's business interests in China on the potential influence of the Chinese government over Twitter through Elon Musk, ultimately concluding that such influence would "probably not" occur. Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates expressed concerns about Musk's willingness to permit the dissemination of public health misinformation, particularly regarding vaccines. Meanwhile, Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales posited that Twitter's performance under Musk's management could either flourish or decline within the next five years. Bitcoin investor Roger Ver and Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong expressed support for the acquisition, highlighting the prospect of diminished perceptions of censorship on the platform. Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google and Alphabet, articulated hopes for Twitter's improvement over time, acknowledging its societal significance, while Mark Zuckerberg, founder and CEO of Facebook and Meta Platforms, conveyed confusion and uncertainty regarding the proposed acquisition. Edgett advised employees against commenting on the alleged cancellation; nonetheless, several Twitter employees shared humorous remarks about the situation. Following Musk's announcement, Tesla shares experienced an increase of 2.11 percent. Former President Trump criticized Musk, labeling the acquisition as "rotten," which subsequently sparked an ongoing dispute between the two figures. Notably, with the exception of Trump, a majority of conservatives aligned with Musk, with former White House chief strategist Steve Bannon condemning Twitter for purportedly misrepresenting the prevalence of spambot accounts, and Turning Point USA CEO Charlie Kirk speculating that Musk may have intended to "expose" Twitter all along.

On October 27, 2022, following a protracted period characterized by indecision, legal disputes, and contentious public exchanges, Elon Musk acquired ownership of Twitter.

On October 28, 2022, an offer of $44 billion (£38 billion) was presented for Twitter under the stipulation of a take-it-or-leave-it arrangement. The board of Twitter initially declined this proposal, subsequently implementing a strategy often referred to as a "poison pill."

On October 28, 2022, Elon Musk acquired Twitter, subsequently terminating the positions of the Chief Executive Officer and several other senior executives. This acquisition has prompted considerable speculation regarding the motivations behind Musk's decision to purchase Twitter and the implications thereof.

On October 28, 2022, Elon Musk assumed control of Twitter through a transaction valued at $44 billion. This development raises questions regarding the future direction of Twitter under Musk's leadership. Additionally, an exploration of the methods by which the world's wealthiest individual acquired Twitter is warranted.

On April 26, 2022, one may inquire into the motivations behind Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter. It can be posited that his actions are influenced by a sincere set of political and social beliefs regarding the platform's role and impact.
